name: Al Shahi Palace
formatted_address: 4235 12 Mile Rd, Warren, MI 48092, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
Ramadan at Al Shahi Palace is always special for me. Since they offer buffet only during Ramadan, I never miss it.
Everything was fresh and flavorful, from the vibrant fruit salad to the rich haleem and soft naan. The 65 biryani and Mango Chicken were absolutely delicious. And I am forever a fan of their jilapi and dahi are the perfect sweet ending.
A truly satisfying and memorable Iftar experience every year.

I was delighted with my experience here! Came in at lunch to try the tikka masala chicken with basmati rice as well as the garlic cheese naan. I was met by Iqbal who represented the restaurant greatly! It was quiet at the time but food was very delicious and fresh! Thank you so much for the delightful experience!

The  food was soo good.  It was my first t ime here with my mother. We really enjoyed the food..specially the jalebi. It totally tasted like dhaka street jalebi. Can't wait till ramadan to come here. Each and every item tasted soo good. The smell was so fresh and crispy.  Thet gave us free cup dohi.  It was a good time. For a bit I was lost and feeling like I'm in dhaka Bangladesh

Quality service with great food. Really pleased with the service provided by the staff. We have a nut allergy in the family, and they were very thorough to make sure the food we took was nut free. It was greatly appreciated.We took a variety of items. The chicken tandoori and both biryanis ( chicken and beef) were our favorite. Eager to come back again and try new items
The food here is amazing, the staff is so nice and helpful, and the restaurant is decorated so beautifully! I really do enjoy coming here with my family! They even have a prayer room!

name: Phulkari Punjabi Kitchen
formatted_address: 27707 Dequindre Rd, Madison Heights, MI 48071, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM

top_reviews.text: Excellent authentic Punjabi Food. The service is great as well. The thali is fantastic and so is makke ki roti - sarson ka saag.

If you’re craving traditional Punjabi cuisine that hits every taste bud, Phulkari Punjabi Kitchen in Madison Heights is the place to be! The flavors here are nothing short of amazing. The Papdi Chaat is a perfect blend of textures and spices, and the Halwa Puri is simply to die for. I would travel light years just to savor these dishes again. Truly, this spot is a must-visit for anyone seeking an authentic and unforgettable dining experience. Wow!

Used to be one of the best Indian restaurant around here. They seem to have completely given up. Maybe it's the second generation who has to taken over, they seem to be genuinely upset to receive a call or a customer walk in. The food quality has slid down. The last time I went in there they did not have forks. I had ordered a seekh kebab and when I requested for one the owner said they don't have forks and anyway I will eat the naans with my hands so why do I need a fork!!!  Poor restaurant cleanliness, food quality sliding down and one of the rudest owner family - can't recommend this to anyone anymore.

Customer service was great and not rushed at all. I ordered lamb curry with rice , 2 veggie samosas, and a 4pc chicken tandoori with naan bread. Total of about $26 gets me enough food to eat for lunch. The chicken tandoori came in all cuts. Wing, leg, thigh and breast. I have been to many indian resturaunts and this is the first time i was offered tandoori in breast or wing. The wing was great, the thigh was awsome, and the leg was phenomenal. Dipping it in the green sauce with a little of the amazingly fresh naan bread was very tasty. The chicken had a grillled char flavor that was very nice. The breast was super dry and pink at the bone🤔. The lamb curry sauce was superb. Must have been one of the best I ever had. The lamb however was a bit over cooked, not all but most. The ones that were not over cooked tasted great. The rice was very nice and moist , exactly how I like it.  Now for the samosas. I normally like to order meat samosas and if they dont have I will accept chicken. They didnt have either one so i went for the veggie ones. They tasted amazing. Very good. Dipped in the green sauce it was wow. The brown sauce was the BEST sauce i have had to this date at any indo/pak/bengali resturaunt. I dont know whats in it but it tasted like a syrupy cumin like sauce. So good that i made sure my last bite was dipped in it. I ordered a side of mango pickel. Its very rare yo find this nowadays and i was super excited to have it. It wasnt as good as the ones i buy from the supermarket but it was ok for being able to have it at a resturaunt. I will def come back and try other dishes and another order of samosas with that awsome brown sauce. I recomend this place but ask them not to over cook the lamb and not undercook part of the chicken.

Good place to have a quick Indian bite. Affordable items on the list which shows on the quantity of food on plate but tastes really good for the money.
They close dine area 30 mins before their closing time, so if you're running late, plan on taking your food home.

name: Mithai Club
formatted_address: Yemans St, Hamtramck, MI 48212, USA

top_reviews.text: Ordered from Mithai club. Very satisfied with the product and the quality of the sweet. Would definitely recommend to friends and family.

name: Spice 21 (Warren)
formatted_address: 30758 Ryan Rd, Warren, MI 48092, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 1:30 PM, 2:30 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: My family and I just dined in, we have the chicken tikka masala, beef curry, tandoori mixed grill with butter naan and garlic naan.  Everything was so amazing and we got it l extra spice level and the it was done to perfection MashaAllah! The chicken was so tender, perfect flavors. This underground place is so much better than those mainstream Indian restaurant places and the prices are not too bad either. Really wholesome service as well :))

We often visit with our family, and our kids absolutely love the food. My husband and I always enjoy our time there—the food is delicious and everything tastes great! Specially the food is always fresh!

The food was absolutely amazing from start to finish. Everything tasted fresh and full of flavor, and you can tell a lot of care went into the preparation. The portions were generous, and the presentation looked just as good as it tasted. The service was friendly and quick, which made the whole experience even better. I would definitely come back again and recommend it to anyone looking for a great meal.
Very Great customer service…Food is Very good.Outstanding ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️

Food was flavorful, hot, and fresh. The boneless chicken biryani was so good!! The cilantro chutney was AMAZING!!! It came with the spring rolls appetizer. We also liked the aloo gobi (potatoes and cauliflower in a tomato based sauce) but felt it needed a bit more flavor. That may be because we ordered it mild and it's usually spicy.  We will DEFINITELY be back!!

name: Pink Garlic Indian Cuisine-Oak Park
formatted_address: 26661 Coolidge Hwy, Oak Park, MI 48237, USA
hours: Monday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
4.7/5 – This spot has become my go-to for butter chicken and chicken tikka. The flavors are consistently rich, creamy, and perfectly spiced, and the portions are generous. The naan is a great size and pairs well with the dishes, though I do wish it was a bit more affordable.
Everything travels well for takeout and arrives fresh. The only downside is that parking can be limited, so you may need to park in the nearby 7-Eleven plaza. Overall, a reliable and satisfying choice when I’m craving quality Indian food, and I’ll definitely keep coming back.

What a great cozy place providing magnificent dishes.excellent food great people
Being an Indian myself, I know what is a good quality Indian food and the importance of balancing spice level. Pink garlic doing a phenomenal job on this.
I have been a customer to that place since 2022. My wife and I order every other week and always been trying different dishes combinations. We were not disappointed NOT A SINGLE TIME !!

We have been amazed by the consistency, quality and service each and every time. Needless to say, the price is extremely reasonable especially compare to other local places they over charge customers with lower quality than Pink Garlic.
I am eagerly waiting to try their two newly open dine in places in Detroit.

We are impressed by the great food every time and their amazing customer service. Highly recommend carry out and catering service for small to medium party.
Keep returning never disappoints us

I ordered a lamb vindaloo and a veg wrap.  Both of them were good.  I would visit again.
For the information, it is a takeout only location.  Because of its fast rotation (with advance order), they make up disadvantage of the limited parking space.

Always a pleasure visiting here! The people tending the counter are so kind and understanding. They will talk to you like you have known each other from previous times. The food is always hot and tasty. My go to meal is butter chicken medium spice or medium plus with added veggies, garlic cheese naan, vegetable samosas, and a mango lassi. 😋 So good. The garlic cheese naan is like no one else. I love it! Highly recommended.

I have loved Pink Garlic for a long time and hope things improve. After experiencing a few issues with take out lately, today we received a large piece of beef and one random piece of Tofu in the Chana Masala which is listed on menu as a Vegan item and does not contain tofu. This shouldn’t be happening. I blame the demand of Doordash, seems to really be hurting a lot of small, local businesses and I am praying for quality improvement.

formatted_address: 3067 Walton Blvd., Auburn Hills, MI 48326, USA

top_reviews.text: Wonderful local Indian grocery with a great selection of products. Fresh gulab jamun and samosa were excellent. Can’t wait to return!

I’ve had nothing but positive experiences at this grocery store, the cashier is extremely warm and friendly. In certain situations, they’ve even kept the store open beyond normal business hours upon request. The price is reasonable and the new renovation is nice. If they could add to their collection of fresh vegetables and fruits it would be great.
Small but cozy. They carry almost everything at decent prices. Try their spicy somasas. So good.
Very nice and clean store. Everything is priced very recently. Definitely will be coming back!

Scratched expiry dates . Whole shelf of just expired products and even expired products mixed up the normal stuff. Staff does not care if it is expired or not. Just making sales what ever the quality is.

name: Chai Culture Café
formatted_address: 896 S Rochester Rd, Rochester Hills, MI 48307, USA
hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: Green Cup Café / Chai Culture is one of our new favorite spots. Tried a bunch of drinks, cookies, and cakes here, and everything has been consistently delicious. The drinks taste great and unique, and the desserts are the kind you actually want to finish. The vibe is relaxed and welcoming, and the staff is always friendly and helpful. Definitely worth checking out if you’re nearby.
Great coffee, amazing lattes, and tasty snacks! Everything is consistently fresh and well made. A perfect place to stop for a quick treat or a cozy break. Thanks for the extra treat!

I was excited to try this individually owned café in the area and really wanted to support a local business. The vibe was okay — they’ve put effort into making the space cute — but it didn’t fully come together for me.

We ordered chai, samosas, and a vegetable panini. The chai was fine but tasted like a standard chai latte you’d get at a big chain. The samosas seemed similar to the frozen ones you find at Indian grocery stores, and they were undercooked and pale instead of crisp and golden. They also didn’t come with any chutney or ketchup, which made them feel even more basic for the price.

The panini was the biggest disappointment. It was made with slices of store‑bought sourdough and filled with canned ingredients like artichokes, olives, and sun‑dried tomatoes. Because of the brine, it tasted very salty and didn’t feel fresh or thoughtfully prepared. Paying around $12 for something assembled mostly from canned goods didn’t feel worth it.

We paid about $27 for the food, and unfortunately the quality and value just weren’t there.

I hope they improve over time because the concept is nice and the area could use more local cafés, but based on this visit, the food didn’t meet expectations.”

This is the best place to get chai, and their food and desserts are wonderful as well. The folks who work here are also always so nice.

A great spot for coming with friends or getting some work done! (They also have a BTS display shelf that I recently noticed and am fond of.) All around my favorite spot to get a nice cup of chai and vibe.

The atmosphere was calming and peaceful. Music was kept at a nice and soft level, which helped me focus on my work. The decoration and furniture were lovely. The staff were all friendly and helpful when I asked questions. The food and drinks were excellent. Will be returning. I got their matcha and I loved it. My sister got an iced brown sugar lavender something (I am sorry I do not remember the name), but she really enjoyed it. I also got a butter chicken sandwich that I liked! Their cookies were also so very delicious!! Pistachio chocolate chip and strawberry white chocolate were flavorful and yummy.

name: Chai Village
formatted_address: 44380 Cherry Hill Rd, Canton Township, MI 48187, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

top_reviews.text: I recently visited Chai Village and had a somewhat mixed experience. The ambience was definitely one of the highlights it was clean, well-maintained, and had a cozy vibe that makes it a nice place to relax or catch up with friends. I really liked the punugulu and chutney; they were fresh, crispy, and flavorful. However, the benne dosa did not meet my expectations, as the taste and texture were not as authentic as I hoped. I was particularly disappointed with the masala chai, which tasted quite off and wasn’t enjoyable. Overall, while the ambience was good, the food quality felt inconsistent and could definitely be improved.🙂🙂

Ordered mirchi bajji, but ironically there was no mirchi inside—just the batter. The taste was below average and didn’t meet expectations. Ambience is nice but the place feels quite congested.
The biggest downside was the wait time; it took over 40 minutes just to get this order. Overall, an okay experience but definitely needs improvement in food quality and service speed.

My feedback was shared based on my personal experience during that visit and was intended to be honest and constructive. I understand that unexpected issues can occur, especially for growing businesses, and I respect the passion and hard work that goes into building a local café.

I appreciate your hospitality and your commitment to improving and growing. Wishing you continued success, and I hope things run more smoothly moving forward.

We had ordered an Eggless Vanilla Buttercream cake recently. It was Awesome. They made the cake with our expected design within a short notice, and had on time delivery too. The cake was delicious and we received a couple of compliments for its design and flavor from our friends. The intricate details they had provided in packaging is also commendable.

Apart from the cake, the authentic Indian food that is being served from street food varieties to parathas to dosa/idly are very tasty and reasonably priced. Mix bhajji, Aloo Paratha and Chola Bhatura are always on our menu along with Masala tea. The people over here are friendly too. It is a must visit cafe for our son during the weekends.

Solid 5. When I first visited, I had a Vada Pav and it was so good.
I was so impressed with everything about this Cafe that I hosted my birthday party here.

Kedar Ji was so kind, so gracious and took care of everything. I am glad I choose to host my birthday here.
I highly recommend this place. Food, hospitality everything is top notch ❤️

I usually take photos of my food, but didn't this time because everything was so delicious, I barely had time to click photos! It goes to show how great this place is!

Absolutely epic food, right here in Canton, heck even across the state of Michigan! Not an understatement when they mention people coming in all the way from Kalamazoo or Benton Harbor for this kind of authentic taste & flavor!

The owners make everyone feel right at home, whether it be by helping through the menu, stopping by and checking on how the food is, or taking care of their guests like family, this place makes you experience how cafe & dhaba culture back home really is like!

What we had and how we rate them:
Peri Peri Fries - 10/10 - Nice hint of peri peri powder, on crisp and well done potato fries Kadak Chai - 10/10 - You can actually taste the essence of the tea leaves and the thickness of the concoction
Maska Bun - 99/10 - Been a while since I've had such an amazing maska bun, heck even the ones in India fall flat many times. This here was epic, buttery, sweet and melted in my mouth! Absolute flavor bomb, packed in an appropriate portion size! One wont do enough; order a bunch and feel satisfied!
Pav Bhaji - 9/10 - Simple, homely, flavorful with the right mix of spices and butter
Benne Dosa - 99/10 - Reminds you of exactly how food back in Bengaluru is! The spice mix, the crispness of the dosa complemented by its soft inner texture, the sambar that is simply out of the world, and the benne aka butter to top it off! Heavenly flavor, authentic af; been to many Kannada restaurants across the US, and this has to take the winners medal for the best authentic benne dosa!
Alfredo Pasta - 9/10 - Simple, cozy, cheesy for those cold Michigan nights
Molten Lava Cake - 10/10 Served in style, with a nice mushy warm soft chocolate cake, and a cone full of chocolate ice cream melting away at the cake, to come home and pack an insane flavor to finish the meal off!

What we would definitely recommend based on what we ate/saw:
Benne Dosa
Malai Bun
Pav Bhaji
Thali & tiffins - Will try next time
Sambar Rice - Will try next time
Veggie Maharaja Burger - Will try next time
Bombay Sandwich - Will try next time
Did I mention benne dosa and malai bun!
And much more...

Thanks again for a great time! Please keep doing this for everyone in Michigan, that wants to experience what being home in India feels like, right in the heart of Michigan, USA!

Loved this new restaurant! The food was fresh and full of flavor, the atmosphere was really nice, and the staff were friendly and welcoming. Definitely a great addition to the area. 🩵
Absolutely delicious food, and fantastic service. An all around perfect experience. Highly recommend!
Great taste, high quality, and excellent service. The staff really looks after you here. I'll definitely be back!

name: Charminar Biryani House - (Griswold St) Downtown
formatted_address: 1226 Griswold St, Detroit, MI 48226, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: Probably the worst Indian food i’ve ever had in my life. I order lamb/goat jalfrezi everywhere and it has never ever looked like this! The rice was so overcooked that it was inedible mush and the naan was burnt somehow?? It seems like a practical joke. There was so much oil in the jalfrezi that it looked like soup and the “meat” was mostly fat. Never again!! Don’t eat here!!

I was here during annual Saviours’ Convention.  The atmosphere here is so positive.

The food was the 1 of the best I ever had.

I enjoyed it very much with my Son more than anything.

The women were in tune with our desires  and that is wonderful.

Thank you for being so gracious!

Ordered Chicken Dum Biryani and Mutton Dum Biryani spicy, both were delicious and so tasty. After long time had chance of eating a pure Dum style biryani and the flavors are just pure. For sure this will be my to go restaurant for future. Food quantity was perfect and fits one full person. Their tea was soooo flavorful and tastes just like the tea in India, We thought the tea was complimentary, which is not and had 2 glasses, but the owner understood and waved off the bill. We truly appreciate her being nice and understanding. We owe her many more visits in future.
The food's really good . Definitely coming back again. Awesome staff as well. The owner personally came in to let me know he's making sure every thing on my order is fresh.

I’m here visiting Detroit from Chicago and I must say this place did not disappoint! The food was amazing and extremely flavorful. I got goat curry, biryani rice, and garlic naan! The lady at the register with the hijab was extremely nice! Helpful with what to order when it came to recommendations. And her service was spectacular! 5/5 service! And she had a nice smile! :)

top_reviews.text: I just moved to michigan, and am TERRIFIED of trying new eyebrow places. However, I did! I AM SO HAPPY! My eyebrows were super uneven due to previous threaders/waxers. She did such an amazing job shaping them. Amazing salon, amazing people! Definitely coming back!
Krishna does an amazing job threading my eyebrows. She is quick and gentle. My eyebrows always turn out perfect.

Worst haircut service I ever received. Rita Mikha was my stylist and it was my first time with them, I requested for just the trim as my previous stylist from another city had given me a perfect haircut. Rita cut my hair unevenly leaving one side hair longer than the other side. When I noticed I went back for correction, that time she tried to make it even but I still feel that my hair is not cut symmetric with few strands longer..Worst experience even after paying the huge amount for a trim

I absolutely love Krishna Salon for my eyebrow threading! I have been a customer for at least 10 years and I have always been happy with my service. Even when I am out of town and have to get my eyebrows done, no one else’s work compares in my opinion. Thank you Krishna!

I've been going to Krishna for my eyebrow threading for years and don't trust anywhere else with them. I live 2 hours away but whenever I'm in town I make sure to schedule an appointment to get them threaded, she's the BEST! Happy also does eyebrows and is great! I've brought friends and family with me to get their eyebrows threaded and they were amazed by Krishna. Cannot recommend this place enough for eyebrow threading!

This hair is beautiful! I shop at Sistah’s Indian Hair Closet often and the quality of hair and customer service is top notch. The owner is very knowledgeable about the industry. She is very helpful when educating clients about the hair. All the way from the personalized customer service to the product itself, it’s a delight. I will continue to buy from Shelonda for as long as she is in this business. When it comes to hair, you want it to look healthy and natural. This is it, its money well spent.

I will not get my hair from anyone else! I absolutely loveeeee them! The hair never shed or tangles! Bomb prices and deals! And the owners are always nice, professional, && helpful! 10/10 HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

Nice welcoming business!!!! Great quality hair, hair looks flawless and lasts a very long time. NO Shedding, Reused my hair three times. Also don’t have a bad smell to it. Comes pre washed. I also have had their 613 hair and it doesn’t shedding feels good and color great.

Love these guys!! Been wearing wigs for over a decade and their hair quality is super nice and soft, less tangling than other wigs, and the cap construction is super well done. They gave me a discount, let me test their hair, overall amazing customer service. Thank you Sistahs!

top_reviews.text: Save your time and money.... do not shop here.   The lady (I believe she is the owner of the store) was very unprofessional.   I came to her needing a dress for a weekend wedding and she assured me a blouse I needed would be ready to pick up on time.  I paid her upfront and trusted she would follow through on her assurance.  I called ahead to let her know what time I would be there and she said she would be there with my blouse.   Well, not only was she not there but she said the blouse could not be made.  SHE TELLS ME THIS JUST HOURS BEFORE THE EVENT!  And then tries to get me to take a completely non- matching blouse that doesn't even fit!  I cannot believe someone could be this unprofessional!  I'm not surprised their store appears to be falling apart, as they don't know a thing about customer service.   Do yourself a favor and stay away from this place!
Good

top_reviews.text: Great place!  Renee is awesome!  She takes great care of my husband and I. She's always so considerate with my husband and his back. Especially when it comes to leaning back and sitting up from getting his hair washed!  We won't go anywhere else!

Chloe was amazing & very understanding when it came to me & my sensitive skin! My eyebrows look amazing, I will be back!! Great Customer Service 👏🏽👏🏽👏🏽

top_reviews.text: I’ve always loved getting my brows threaded here they are quick and do a good job until recently there has been a different lady she does a horrible job. I’ve seen her twice the first time she was just sloppy and left hairs that i had to go home and pluck. Why would i spend 12$ to get my eyebrows done just to go home and fix them?? The second time she was also sloppy left hair and each brow looks completely different and she cut me right above my eye then proceeded to say it was because my skin is thin (i’ve never had this problem before) will not be returning

You be the judge of the brows…. Anyway the facility is relatively clean and pleasant women working there;  their sanitation is questionable, i didn’t see anyone wash their hands or use hand sanitizer between clients, I didn’t see them clean seats between each client. I received an underarm wax and the person used the same dip stick over and over in the pot; I did not see her change the wax and do not know if that was new or used wax. She did use gloves during wax. There is a linen spread on the table for waxes, unknown if linen is changed between guest or not . In hindsight i should not have proceeded with my wax and threading. That said my underarm wax is fine  and will come back to update if any weird fungi/unusual rashes occur.

I had to delete my review on how this place deserves recognition,
Only one person here deserves recognition.

Edit. Since then, I have only gone to see Rukmani. She is amazing and listens to me. I've been trying to grow my eyebrows out but still have them presentable. I went here on my usual Sundays to see her and Nadia ( I did not get her card, I am not sure how to spell her name) another lady was there and I should of asked for my usual lady because now I have never felt uglier. She basically took off all of my eyebrows,  they are super thin and uneven and I went home, and my Finace literally said I should shave them off and just redraw them back on they would look better. I feel like a CLOWN. Then. As if she wanted to fix her mistake, she just tried to sell me makeup, as if that's what I needed. No, I need them to pay for eyebrow replacement or tattoos or something.  I went home and plucked them a little to get them to at least look similar. I am lost now. I have never had this experience. It took months of work to grow my eyebrows, and it took less than an hour of her not caring at all. I am disgusted, and now I am forever surprised. The only miracle happening is me not going back and raising hell. Which I should. I don't want my money back. I just want them to know. I have never had a problem, but this is terrible.

Out of all the times I’ve came this was by FAR the best experience. Farwa is an absolute perfectionist. I was amazed, she literally got my eyebrows symmetrical and they STILL look thick and even more defined. I’m very impressed. She has hands of an artist. An eyebrow sculptor if you will!

My eyebrows were done completely uneven, she waxed half of one eyebrow off and left a hole in one. She cut too much off the top and was trying to have me leave w left over wax still on my face. When I told her I didn’t like it, she said well it’s wax… I have gotten my eyebrows waxed for 25 years and it’s never looked like this. Plus you offer waxing, it’s a service. Trying to blame it on me because you’re horrible at your job. Don’t go here.

hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: I recently had the pleasure of dining at Masala King Restaurant, and it was an absolutely delightful experience. The food was incredibly tasty and truly awesome! Each dish was bursting with flavor, showcasing the rich and authentic spices that Indian cuisine is known for. The staff was friendly and attentive, making sure that our dining experience was top-notch. The ambiance of the restaurant was warm and inviting, perfect for a cozy meal with friends or family. I highly recommend Masala King Restaurant to anyone looking for a memorable and delicious dining experience. I'll definitely be coming back for more!

Came in from out of town for a protest and needed food after. Masala King hit the spot after a long day outside the chicken kurma and the flavor was so perfect and creamy. They can make most things in a range of spice levels so everyone can enjoy. Anwar waited on us and was so friendly and amazing make sure to chat if you see him!

Very disappointed. Worst buffet ever. The food was too old and we didn’t realize until we start eating. I think only chicken tikka and naan was fresh and rest of the items were old. The vegetables had bad smell and tasted tangy. The biriyani didn’t even look like biriyani, it was like they added few pieces of chicken from curry and added with rice. We had stomach upset after having their food. I’m leaving review so they can fix the issue and serve fresh food so people can go back and enjoy the food. The guy who was upfront was very nice and the environment was amazing but their food was old and tasted so bad. PLEASE do not serve old food. I honestly don’t give reviews unless it’s too good or too bad.

I absolutely love everything offered. Individual meals are ENORMOUS and the lunch buffet is an incredible combination if value and price. My only complaint is the price is too high to justify going more often. I hope an owner or manager sees this and understands it's from someone who genuinely wants to visit more: Can you offer a lunch menu with smaller portions and price (even half sized meal for $7-8 would get me coming at least once per week)? Sadly, a $13 entree, plus $2-3 if I wanted nuances, is a bit much these days.

Today We went to this restaurant for Iftar buffet. Unfortunately this is very disappointing to say that their food was not good enough.As a Muslim we expect a decent Iftar .But I think they serve old food those food taste lack flavor and cold .They didn’t even serve dates !! We had to asked for dates !

One of their staff member was also rude!
Overall I think they should improve their service and food quality .

top_reviews.text: Delicious food. Catered for our family event. Quantity was good. Quality was fresh. Taste was authentic & delicious. Will definitely order again.
They catered my wedding and the food was amazing. Everyone gobbled it up. The ghulab jamun, the biryani, the qorma, all top tier. Highly recommend.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
I love the food at Naan Stop Grill! Every time I order, the food is always fresh and delicious. The chicken biryani and chicken seekh kabob are especially amazing — full of flavor and cooked perfectly. Definitely one of my favorite spots!

I really do not know where to begin. I placed an order for a family brunch and really thought that they would not be able to accommodate me due to being a small order and them being so busy, BUT Naan Stop Grill went above and beyond and treated me like family and made the most amazing food my family and I have ever tasted. Whilst picking up the order, they also gave me a sample of their butter chicken which FYI is my wife's favorite and mentioned it was the best she has ever tasted. They are officially my go to and will have my business. Only thing I must mention is that they landed me into a food coma from eating so much but it was well worth it. So if you want the best authentic Pakistani homemade food this is the place to order.

They catered food for my daughter's wedding!  The food was very delicious, ALL our guests liked the food and the service provided by the staff.
The quality and quantity of the food was beyond our expectations.  They were there on time and stayed until required.  They were efficient and courteous; they neatly packed all the left over food and drinks for us to take home!
We would definitely recommend them to all our relatives and friends!

name: Noorjahan Indian Grill (Mack/Cadieux)
formatted_address: 16624 Mack Ave, Grosse Pointe Park, MI 48230, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: We had a wonderful dining experience at this restaurant! The tandoori chicken was absolutely outstanding — juicy, full-flavored, and cooked to perfection. The spices hit just right, and every bite was delicious. What really stood out was the customer service: the staff were friendly, attentive, and genuinely wanted to make sure we had a great meal. From the warm welcome to the helpful recommendations and prompt service, we felt truly taken care of. Highly recommend this place for anyone craving great Indian food and great hospitality — can’t wait to come back!

For over three years, I have been coming to Noorjahan, and every time I feel like I am the hungriest man alive. The food is phenomenal and the taste is the best Indian food I have ever had at any Indian restaurant. I used to come with my roomate almost once a week, and now i make sure to have this restaurant in my navigation for any bigger get togethers with friends.

Shout out to Salma, she is an amazing waitress and very personable.

I am sad to be moving away from Michigan, but any time I come back to the state I will make sure to visit this store.

We were in the mood for some butter chicken and didn’t wanna make the drive to our go to spot on the other side of town. New Little India at 23 & Van Dyke.

Noorjahan is the second place we’ve tried on this side of town.

Food was delivered hot. Everything we ordered was present & accounted for.

Two orders of butter chicken, one mild, one medium. The kick was noticeable in the medium. Chicken was tender, sauce was good.

The rice was fresh & warm.

Naan. We snagged a regular garlic naan & a cheese naan. Both were good.

I’m noticing a big difference flavor wise from my go to place & these Eastside Indian restaurants. Depth of flavor? Where is it?

I hate to take a star a way, but I’m to the point where I can heat up a store bought butter chicken sauce, add my own chicken & serve it over basmati rice I make at home and if I put it in a to go container, it could pass as restaurant quality these days.

The Naan, average. Little garlic, little oil. A few specks of fresh herbs & Im supposed to be ok with paying 12 plus dollars for a two orders of Naan?

I’m paying 50$ dollars for butter chicken & naan for two and it’s average at best. Take some time with that butter chicken man, develop those flavors. The Naan, it’s not an after thought….. it’s the co star of this dish. Treat it as such.

Still searching for the my go to spot on the Eastside.

20 out of 10 experience no exaggeration

From the moment we walked in the energy was warm and welcoming. The waitress was so kind attentive and genuinely sweet. She made us feel like family and even surprised us with a free soup 🥣 and let me tell you that soup was absolutely delicious

As a vegan I was blown away by the flavor and variety of options. Every dish I had was seasoned to perfection and so satisfying 😋. My family couldn’t stop raving about the shrimp 🍤 they loved it

And the portions? Huge 🍛 We left with bags of leftovers. Definitely worth every penny and then some

If you’re looking for amazing Indian food a cozy vibe and top tier service Noorjahan Indian Grill is the spot. I’ll definitely be back again and again ❤️

Fantastic food. Ordered online, picked up quickly and easily. Big portions. There are lots of options for spice level on dishes. Take them seriously! I didn’t and my “hot” dish was definitely spicy. But delicious!! Next time I order, I’ll be ordering a bit lower level.
