There was a ROACH in my food! How many must be lurking in their kitchen? Unfortunately I took a few bites before I noticed. YUCK 🤢 Stay away unless you like extra protein. They may want to put a disclaimer on their “vegetarian” menu!

Best Indian food in Birmingham.The most authentic Butter Chicken and Chicken Tikka Masala, as if straight from Old Delhi, the heart of India.Their quality and quantity both are great even if I take away.
Truly lives up to its name 😊One can feel the quality and freshness of the food. Loved butter chicken and vegetable samosa. It was simply amazing
Really good food great service overall awesome me and my bro loved it

name: Taj Cuisine Indian Pak food
formatted_address: 28544 Ford Rd, Garden City, MI 48135, USA
hours: Monday: 1:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 1:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 1:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 1:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 1:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 2:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 2:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: This is my absolute favorite place to eat! I’ve been eating here for the past nearly 15 years. The staff are friendly and whenever I eat there I feel like I’m visiting family because that’s how great they treat their customers at this great restaurant. If you go check out the lentil soup and Butter Shrimp. Two of my favorite dishes.

Absolutely delicious! The garlic naan was perfect, the tandoori chicken was amazing, and my fellow diners really enjoyed the chicken tikka masala and lamb vindaloo. Also really good - the samosas. Great service as well.
My favorite place to get Chicken and Lamb Biryani, always get HOT and love it. Food isnt oily like I have had at other places. Staff is always super friendly and nice!

This place was so good!  You get so much food, and it's easy to order lots of dishes to share.  They had a lot of options, and everything was so flavorful.  The naan was fresh and perfect 👌. I would definitely recommend.

Very underrated, the portions are generous and delicious, the naan is huge compared to most Indo/pak restaurants and its just really good honestly, I got the butter paneer with potatoes this time, and I was able to split 1 single order with my wife, and save our second order for later!

name: Sakura Desi Cuisine
formatted_address: 3635 Rochester Rd, Troy, MI 48083, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 5:00 – 5:30 PM | Sunday: Closed

top_reviews.text: Pretty authentic. The food was pretty good. Maybe it is just me being a mom who lacks sleep. But I found it hard to follow along with the waitress. I have really no idea what we ordered after a while. She would come up and put something on our table and we would say thank you and it was then added to our bill. We ended up with a ton of food to go home with. I do have to take a star away for cleanliness. The bathrooms needed attention and it looked like the place could use a good cleaning. But it was overall a good experience.

First impression: when I was driving up to this place it looked a bit sketchy. Once I got inside it seemed a bit odd with some looking paint
The wait: we had to wait about 2 minutes for an employee to greet us after that we waited for approximately 10 minutes
Food: The food was fantastic and was extremely entertaining to watch because we did the hibachi style. Great desert
The Price: Expensive but worth it it was 15-45 dollars
Bathrooms: male and female only but they were extremely clean and quiet modern
Overall: A fun place to bring you family or friends, great food, okay service and I would rate it 8.9/10 stars just for the wait at the beginning and in-between meals
very nice place if you wanna have a get together or a large party. food was really good too

Hosted a party here recently. The food was good, hall was big and staff was friendly.  However a couple dishes were not refilled, not even once. This was a bit disappointing as some of the guests did not even get to try all the food items we ordered.

Re: thank you for reaching out. July 16th.
The chicken tenders and dessert.
Best Asian food! flavors were balance, clean and fresh ingredients it looks like, good for every taste in abundance presentation, Service was really nice! We will come back for sure! Yummy!!

name: MADINA Indian Restaurant
formatted_address: 33323 Harper Ave, St Clair Shores, MI 48082, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM

top_reviews.text: Wish I would've stopped in here sooner! The butter chicken is so good, and our curry chicken was excellent. Definitely order the garlic and cheese naan bread!
The food was amazinggggg for the price. I’d go as far to say my new favorite in the area. Great flavor. Great portion. And excellent customer service. Will definitely be my new spot for lunch breaks!

My boyfriend and I visited here today for our first time!!! The service was genuine, positive and inviting!!! We were greeted with a warm smile, helped very much with the menu and questions we had. The food is all around outstanding and scrumptious with inticing look,smell and taste!!! The portion sizes are actually pretty big!!! We are vegetarian and the menu has many options to accommodate. We got the vegetarian Aloo Gobi, vegetarian Bindi Marsala (both came with white rice),8 HUGE garlic naan, vegetarian Samosa (also HUGE pieces) with two savory and  delectable sauces!!! This restaurant is a MUST TRY!!! Your taste buds and stomachs will be highly satisfied!!! 🤗💛✌️🕉️☮️☯️💯

I received this dish from Doordash; however, there were at least 5 bones in it, and I had just started eating it.This was the Madina Special Biryani.Should have a warning because there was a lot of bone  and bone fragments..

We had a delicious lunch. Ordered veg samosa, chicken vindaloo, sheek kabab, mango Lassi and gulab jamun. They are mainly a carry out place but there are 2 tables here if you want to eat. We ate here and the nice gentleman gave us plates for us to eat. Definitely the best Indian food around. I love Indian style food and theirs is exceptional!

name: Kurrys at Troy Indian Cuisine
formatted_address: 3303 Rochester Rd, Troy, MI 48083, USA

top_reviews.text:
We catered from Kurry’s Restaurant yesterday for my son’s birthday, and the food was absolutely delicious! The service was incredibly efficient - the owner was hands-on and diligent in overseeing everything, and the delivery was right on time. The food arrived fresh, well-organized, and beautifully presented.
All of our guests truly appreciated the authentic Indian flavors, and the spice level was perfectly balanced. Portions were also very generous. We will definitely continue catering from Kurry’s for future events and highly recommend them to anyone looking for delicious Indian food in the area!

We had wonderful experiance for the catering service we received. The staff was very accomdating and prepard the food as per our cutomization request. A special thanks to seetharam for outstanding service. He was attentive, polite and made sure all requirements are clearly understood....

We recently had lunch at Kurrys in Troy. We ordered Gongura Tandoori Chicken and Chicken Biryani. The food was really good. The biryani was especially tasty, it had great flavor and just the right amount of spice. We really enjoyed the meal and would definitely go back again.

Wry amazing packet for 20 ppl who we bought 8/22/24. Good amount of food and everyone at my work loves it. Everyone wants more to order and asking the restaurant locations.

I’m vegetarian but ordered chicken dishes 1st time for my team and they loved it.

Staff is very amazing help me to explain the detail of the food. Thank you Kurry’s amazing staff ❤️❤️

KURRYS INDIAN RESTAURANT TROY
👍👍 👍 👎🏿 👎🏿

4/25. Ordered few items after a while. Fried rice, kothu Porotta and lollipop came out decent. Gongura chicken went bad but offered credit at front desk. Not sure we can use it or not. Charging more for credit card or giving discount for cash still there. Not carried cash so end paying more with card 😩

Had a chat with restaurant manager . He had explained what went wrong on memorial day and confirmed service improvements. Updating rating and comments to support local business. we will order again and update 😋

May2023
We are regular customer for a while . Few things noticed lately and changing to 3 stars and will change it back once see the response and progress
1. Carry out packing has to improve . For $83 items all squeezed into one plastic bag. Curries spill and could have avoided with good packing . Call and share the photos to front desk but no response
2. Quantity of the meat was less as it looks like lot of party order and kitchen was hurry.

3. 5% discount offer for cash 😔

Dec 22
All good but chef need to check the Kothuporotta . It cook like mixed vegetables fried and almost no Porotta. You can see the photo. It’s good if chef can check actually how to make yummy kothuporotta😋
Aug 22
AMBIENCE & CLEANLINESS 👍 -  Not top of the line but decently maintained
LOCATION  👍 Right in Rochester Rd and bigbeaver teoy
QUALITY OF THE FOOD- 👍 we ordered chicken majestic, chicken fried rice, chicken curry and Naan  .
VALUE for MONEY👍 Not pricey and priced it good.
SERVICE 👎🏿 server can smile 😩 service was ok but can improve but it's typical for many Indian restaurant.

Sep 2021
Food  and service overall great😋😋😋😋. Chicken majestic and  chicken Tikka masala good. Goat halim was just ok and taste little different. Missing few key ingredients but taste ok. Chef need to revisit this and opportunities for improvement👍Service was good 💪

name: Spice Valley
formatted_address: 49087 Schoenherr Rd, Shelby Township, MI 48315, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:45 AM – 9:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:45 AM – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:45 AM – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:45 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:45 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 11:45 AM – 9:30 PM

top_reviews.text: This was my first time coming here and I believe it was my first time trying Indian food. I love exploring different cuisines and am so happy to have chosen this place to eat. Our waiter was so nice and he offered appetizers off the menu that helped us enjoy our experience even more. The food was delicious and it didn’t take too long or too quick to come out. I really enjoyed the green sauce made with cilantro! The waiter again was very nice and invited us to come back for the buffet on the weekends! Upon walking in we were greeted and sat where we wanted. There was no music playing and it wasn’t a very bright restaurant since they had turned off some lights. It would be the perfect place for big families and the sound of the fish tank helped a lot with the quietness.

Grubhub order, the gobi manchurian was bitter, tasted like old oil. The lamb skewers were tough and had the same bitter(old) oil taste. Virtually no lamb in the vindaloo, was more potatoes than anything as you can see in the photo. First and last time. I did call and was told "oil is changed daily ".  Told her it was virtually inedible, the dishes are bitter. Absolutely is not the case. And as oily as the food is, ruins everything. I would note that there have been a dozen reviews since I left my review and many, if not most are by reviewers with zero or only a few reviews on google. I question how accurate their Google reviews are, elsewhere someone mentioned that same fact in their review. Do not assume 1,103 google reviews and the 4.8 stars are factual.

If I could give more than 5 stars I would! We were greeted as soon as we walked in the door. The restaurant is clean and nicely decorated. We had the Chicken Tikka Massala and Butter Chicken Tikka… mild plus, and Butter Naan. All were delicious and the spice level was perfect!. Our waitress was Great!!! We will definitely be back!

Enjoyed a wonderful and truly authentic Indian dinner with my best friend. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, and our hostess/server Sumaiya made the experience even better. She was friendly, courteous, and spot-on with her recommendations. We’ll definitely be back!

The was very good. The waitress was really nice and very attentive and the price was very reasonable. Main dining room was very clean, looks like they kept the old booth,fish tank and such from the Chinese restaurant. Food was served fast but she gave us time to finish each dish before she brought out the next one. I will definitely go back!

name: BISMILLAH KABOB N CURRY
formatted_address: 4214 E 10 Mile Rd, Warren, MI 48091, USA
hours: Monday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Tuesday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 11:30 PM

top_reviews.text: The food was excellent.  The waiter was extremely helpful.  I’m not too well versed with Indian food but the waiter helped me.  I can’t remember the appetizer but it was so good. I had the chicken tandoori biryani, my daughter had butter chicken (forgot to get a picture) and my husband had coconut chicken and chicken 65. My husband and daughter loved the naan. The bathroom needs some TLC but overall this was a positive experience

Best food in town. Very nice hard-working employees great services a highly recommend giving them a great tip if you can. I had every dish they sell and it's amazing. Thank you guys for your hard work and keep up with amazing job.
Thought I’d give them a chance on my 2nd take-out from them. The shawarma/chicken wrap they made in

They are fooling people with their BakhorKhani. Its literally a paratha from those regular paratha packet. Nothing close to BakorKhani that they sell for $2/each piece. Please stop fooling people at least during the month of Ramadan. Also, their Ramadan Jalebi line is very unorganized and annoyingly slow. I would avoid them at all cost!
we ordered Daalgosht, it was the best thing and taste amazing. All other food items were really great and they ordered some complementary snacks as well.

name: Marigold Indian Cuisine
formatted_address: 20860 Hall Rd, Clinton Township, MI 48038, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM

top_reviews.text: The food was delicious. I got the somosas and ground lamb which was very flavorful. The customer service was excellent and the wait time was short. While you’re waiting on your food you’re surrounded by how fragrant it is. I’ll definitely be back.

It was second or third time for us. We live nearby and love Indian food so it’s a win win 🤩 chicken tikka masala comes with rice and we like to order it together with garlic naan. Everything is delicious, workers are very friendly and nice so I recommend to try it

I had high hopes given the 5 star reviews. Unfortunately, it wasn't very good and the quality of ingredients seemed low. I'd recommend Madina in St Clair Shores or Charminar in Troy/Detroit for authentic and better quality.

Marigold has the greatest Indian food in the area. Friendly, accommodating staff with a positive attitude. Biased towards the chili chicken, however I'd go there just to get a bag of Naan. Nothing but good things to say!
My husband and I ordered takeout for a date night at home. No regrets. The food was delicious. It was my first time trying malai kofta and I can’t wait to eat it again.

name: Grains Indian Cuisine
formatted_address: 1601 S Waverly Rd, Lansing, MI 48917, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

top_reviews.text: Check this (Grains Indian Cuisine) place out. Interior looks so nice and clean (sorry forgot to take photos). More importantly the food and service is great. I recommend the panipuri (yummy goodness in little bites) for the flavor explosion. 🥰 It seems odd at first but it's really good. They gave us tapas (the thin crisps with 2 sauces) which is awesome. We tried the tandoori sampler which is good to see which ones you like. We like the Chicken Tikka the best but the lamb and mint chicken kebab was also pretty good. We split the Korma Chicken and had to have fresh garlic naan. Definitely enjoyed everything.

New Indian Cuisine that just opened not too long ago where the old Flapjack used to be. Finally had the chance to stop by and try them out the other day.
This is an easy 3.5 for me.
Food was good but didn't wow me, but it was good. I had pretty all Indian spot here in Lansing/East Lansing/Okemos etc.
They did a great job fixing up this spot. The inside got to be one of the cleanest Indian spot I'd been to. Looked almost like an upscale restaurant, the staff was dress nicely.
Would I got back to it again absolutely there's still plenty I haven't tried yet on the menu.
The only thing was we had to wait almost an hour before I had to asked if our food was ready yet and then few minutes after it started to come out.

Grains is a great addition to lansing, they are serving fresh authentic Indian food in a stylish atmosphere. Serving sizes are amazing for the price and the service was top notch. I'd highly recommend this place if you are looking for something different.

Loved the service!!! Sam was charming, informative and knowledgeable!  We ordered dal (which I wish would have been offered as a side but isn’t), samosa chat, tika kabobs, vegetable biryani, fish tika, tandoori mixed grill, and mango cake. Also the bartender made a fruity “surprise me” cocktail and a gin lemonade for us. The cocktail menu is not printed yet. He shakes the cocktails just like the movies!! Also white wine. Everything was fabulous. Will definitely be back!

I love this place! Sam and the entire staff are very friendly, and their restaurant is beautiful, but most of all the food is freaking delicious! It quickly became my new favorite Indian restaurant in the greater Lansing area! Go there!

name: Indian Spice
formatted_address: 621 S Mitchell St, Cadillac, MI 49601, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 8:00 PM

top_reviews.text: Indian Spice- wow what a treat!! The restaurant was clean and the staff were very friendly and attentive. The new chef has some real talent!! The food was exceptional!! I ordered chili paneer for an appetizer, which I cleaned the plate clear it was SO GOOD! The garlic naan was perfectly baked and full of flavor. The entree was chicken vindaloo and was bursting with amazing flavors and Chicken was tender . Even the fancy sparkling lemonade I got was soothing and crisp. For desert I had the rice pudding and it was also delightful. Very nice presentation as well:) it’s definitely 5 stars all around. Can’t wait to go back and try their buffet!

First time and was not sure I would like but it was absolutely amazing! I had quite a few dietary restrictions and they were so accommodating! I had the aloo ghobhi - so good!!! Garlic naan is a must! And a conversation with Dave is the best! Definitely coming back!

The food was delicious- my first experience with Indian food. I ordered the butter chicken (red dish) and my husband ordered the chicken korma (green dish) and we both enjoyed them. We also ordered one side of spicy Indian veg and an order of garlic naan. The soda is served in a can with a glass of ice.

So much better since it reopened. The owner brought in chefs from Chicago, greatly expanding the menu options. Service was fantastic! They really care and want feedback. I ordered my dish medium spicy. It was good but still pretty mild. They brought out their homemade hot sauce and, wow! Sooo good. It was everything a hot sauce should be, packing just as much flavor as heat and enhancing the flavor of the dish. It took an already good dish to the next level. Can’t wait to go back.
FYI they’re doing a Thanksgiving day buffet 😃

Update!!!! We came back for dinner after the restaurant temporarily closed then reopened. VERY SATISFIED. the portion sizes are bigger (enough for leftovers!)and the quality is great. Definitely recommend!!

We stopped in for the lunch buffet 😋 it was delicious! We will most certainly be returning for dinner. So glad Cadillac now has an indian restaurant.

name: Indian Heaven Cuisine
formatted_address: 1184 S Lapeer Rd, Orion Township, MI 48360, USA

top_reviews.text: Great experience! We brought a friend who had never had Indian food before and she loved it! The waiter was incredibly helpful in suggesting menu items for us to try. We got the butter chicken, chicken korma, and veg biryani and lots of different naan. All were fantastic! My favorite was the chicken korma. We will definitely be coming back again.

Just ordered a lunch special at around 2.15pm. I walk in earlier than the estimated finishing time and was immediately greeted by a very kind gentleman. He let me know my order was still being prepared and I sat in their waiting area and waited. They had lovely music playing at a good volume. They finished my order about 8 minutes earlier than expected. The food was HOT and i got it to go. Ate it in my car. The food spice level I ordered was a mild, as to not upset my stomach. It was the perfect amount, had all the unique flavourings included also. The heat from the spice was very mild, slight warming on your tongue. The portion size was ideal for one person, might have some left overs depending on how much you can eat. The price was also ideal at 9.99 from 11am to 3pm. The place does close from 2.30pm to 4.30pm which i was not aware of until right now. The location is great, lots of free parking. I would come back and refer others to this place.

Wouldve included a photo of my food but i just ate it all. 🙂

update: included food pic this time!

Online ordering experience was good. Picked up the order right once I was notified it was ready, which was right on schedule. After that, everything went south. Came home to find we had only one small (not even totally filled) side of rice for two entrees (butter chicken and lamb korma). Our naan was over cooked, mostly dark brown. And to add, it had a long hair baked into it...delicious. We also ordered a side of Paneer Pakora, and those were unfortunately solid as a puck and dry as the Sahara. Spice level of our dishes seemed on point with our request, but that's about the only positive I can pull out of this experience.

I ordered one lamb biryani and one goat biryani, and unfortunately the experience was very disappointing. The shop owner did mention that it was not dum biryani, and I agreed since many places serve a similar style. However, what was served did not resemble biryani at all—it was simply plain rice mixed with curry.
The taste was very poor, and this was honestly the worst biryani I have ever had. The rice quality was bad and did not seem like basmati; it felt like regular plain rice. On top of that, the portion size was very small for the price.
Overall, the quality, taste, and quantity were far below expectations. I would not recommend this place for biryani.

Party of Three! Had a delicious dinner  tonight 😋 The staff is super welcoming and provided great service and food recommendations. We cleaned our plates as the food was very tasty from the Samosa, to the Naan bread, and then the main dish. Spices perfect and we all had mild levels 👌 We will be back!

name: Spicebowl Indian Grill and Eatery
formatted_address: 24060 Harper Ave, St Clair Shores, MI 48080, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: Absolutely delicious. Such a peaceful atmosphere. Everything was fresh & came out quickly. Staff was so friendly. 10/10 our favorite place for sure :)

1st time customer and I got the Tandoori Mixed Grill Medium Spice and It was the best combination of meats you can ask for! So delicious and the owner gave me 3 side sample sauces to try since it was my first time trying their food. Raita, Butter Chicken Sauce and the Green Sauce. All three sauces were really good.. I also got the Garlic Naan Bread👌🏼Great Indian Food! I will definitely be back..

Food was really great, but we found that mild was way to spicy for us. My wife got number 36 and I got 44. They tasted pretty much the same but I'd say her's was better. I'm not sure if our server was also working the kitchen, but I probably had the worst service I've had all year at a restaurant. I basically had to demand to finally get some water, and I had to go find my own napkins. It took FOREVER to get our food, but at least it was good. I don't think I'll be coming back, but I might try the buffet.

This is an absolute staple in mine and my buddy's dining spots. Incredibly hospitable, unbelievable food, fair prices. 10/10 no complaints ever in the two dozen visits. Just be aware that the spice levels are really accurate. Medium plus is no joke and that's coming from a spice freak.

Traffic combined with heat convinced me to skip the long drive to my go to spot & to try a new local spot.

We called our order in the old fashioned way, gentlemen who answered the phone was super friendly & even friendlier in person.

Restaurant was completely empty, so we were in & out.

Snagged 2 orders of garlic naan. Tasty but a few pieces were slightly over cooked on the bottom, but I’m a gentleman and ate them so my grrl didn’t have too.

Cheese Naan. Not impressed. A sprinkle of cheddar? Most of it stuck to tin foil.

Butter Chicken. We both got the mild. I didn’t think it was spicy, but my grrl has a different tolerance for heat than me, thats for sure.
I thought the chicken was marinated well, and the sauce was tasty. Fresh herbs, plenty of flavor, great gravy for the steamed basmati rice.

I enjoyed everything, could definitely see myself dining in or ordering door dash now that I’ve had it once. 4 solid stars, leaving room for improvement on future visit.

name: Taste of India
formatted_address: 2383 E Hill Rd, Grand Blanc, MI 48439, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 4:00 – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:30 PM

top_reviews.text:
Please go support this delicious Indian place! We have been looking for so long for a good spot to eat a hood Tikka masala curry and this place didnt disappoint.

They are still new but their service was great and the food was amazing. We ordered freshly made Naan bread it melts in your mouth. The tikka massala was not as greasy as others we have tried, this one was genuinely light but with a perfect herb fresh balance, we ordered chinese-Indian noodles very mild and they were exquisite and a Lentil soup that gives you a warm welcome, so natural and fresh.

We will def be back! The place is absolutely gorgeous so modern and beautiful.
The Bar area is amazing!

They have buffet everyday at 11am.

Finally. A good, solid tasting Indian buffet in Genesee county. I dont know when they opened, but I wish I had known sooner. Everything tasted amazing, ESPECIALLY the butter chicken. The service was spot on, the guy running the dining room was polite, and checked up often to make sure my dining experience was up to par. The atmosphere was quiet, and welcoming with a modern bar located right next to the buffet; I have to admit it's an odd combination of sports bar/Indian buffet, but I like it. I highly recommend if you are looking for Indian food, and don't want to drive an hour south. Thank you Taste of India, I really enjoyed my experience here.

Came here for the buffet at 12:20 and the food was delicious. Fresh and still hot, with a good selection. I highly recommend the buttered chicken and the shrimp masala. The garlic naan is a must have when you’re here, and they will bring it fresh and hot to your table. Their rice kheer was sweet and so balanced. Their cocktails are well mixed with a whole mix of flavor varieties. I had their cardamom tea, and I could drink it by the gallon. The atmosphere is somewhere between classic Indian decor with a sports bar. Very attentive staff and kind smiles. Will be coming here again!

First time visiting and have to say I was impressed from when I first sat down until when we left.  The restaurant has an upscale feeling with a beautiful bar and contemporary seating.  The table settings are classy and include a silver carafe, blue knobby glasses, silver serving dishes and black napkins.  All very nice touches.  The wait staff is prompt and also offered suggestions on dishes.  Our server was very accommodating and prompt. As soon as I was wearing my Malai Kofta on my brand new shirt, the wait staff brought me napkins and didn’t make me feel piggish.  We ordered vegetable pakora, naan bread (1 was enough for two people), malai kofta,  mushroom masala (which came with white rice) and two sodas.  All of the food was phenomenal, spiced nicely and served beautifully.   We left stuffed, satiated and completely satisfied.  We will definitely be returning to try other dishes.  I’m so happy we have an excellent Indian restaurant…finally…in our town.  Bravo!

Something other than pizza, fast food and fried food in Grand Blanc, what a welcome change.  The water comes in a steel carafe and it's pleasantly flavoured with lemon and mint. Complimentary popadums are welcome, they are fresh too. The chicken and curry leaves appetiser highly recommended. I went for mild and it certainly had a tang to it. Portion side was large enough to be an entree. Naan was fresh and crispy, aloo gobi slightly heavy on the tomato sauce but perfectly acceptable fresh and automatic again. Chillen Tikka was excellent. Altogether this was an absolutely five star experience I recommended.

name: Authentikka Indian Cuisine, Banquet Wixom
formatted_address: 49809 Grand River Ave, Wixom, MI 48393, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM

top_reviews.text:
We recently hosted our baby shower here, and couldn't be happier with how everything turned out! The banquet setup was beautiful and provided the perfect atmosphere for our celebration.
The food was absolutely awesome—our guests are still raving about the flavors and quality. What really set this place apart, though, was the service. The owner and the support staff were excellent; they were incredibly supportive, attentive to every detail, and made sure everything ran smoothly so we could actually enjoy the day. If you’re looking for a venue for a special event, I highly recommend them!

Very tasty food and good portion size for the price. First time trying out this place, and this will be my go-to for Indian food from now on. I got the Chettinad lamb, butter chicken, malai kofta, paneer biryani, Hyderabad chicken biryani, chicken hakka noodles, mango lassi and garlic naan. Everything was very good, especially the paneer biryani, malai kofta, butter chicken and mango lassi. One star deduction is because the Chettinad lamb curry was super salty, and the Hyderabad biryani wasn’t that tasty. I just won’t be getting these two items anymore, but I’ll def be back to try different items from the menu.

I recently hosted a family event at this restaurant’s banquet hall, and it was an absolute delight from start to finish. The food was simply outstanding—every dish was flavorful, fresh, and beautifully presented, making it a highlight for all our guests. The service team went above and beyond; they were incredibly helpful, anticipating our needs and ensuring everything ran smoothly without a hitch.
What impressed me most was the extensive choice of menu options, allowing us to customize the spread to suit everyone’s tastes, from vegetarian delights to hearty mains. The hall itself was impeccably ready upon our arrival—spotless, well-organized, and set up exactly as discussed. The ambience added the perfect touch, with elegant lighting, comfortable seating, and a warm, inviting atmosphere that made the evening feel special and relaxed.
I would wholeheartedly recommend this banquet hall to anyone planning an event—whether it’s a wedding, corporate gathering, or birthday celebration. It’s a gem that delivers on every front!

I’ve catered from Authentikka several times now for both corporate events and personal parties, and they’ve been exceptional every single time. Venkat is incredibly responsive and accommodating, even with last-minute requests, he always delivers on time and with great quality. He will make changes and adjust how food is according to how I ask. And always has a solution for requests that are not in norm. The food is consistently delicious, fresh, and reliable, which is why I keep going back. The value for the quality and quantity they provide is outstanding. Highly recommend Authentikka for anyone looking for dependable and flavorful catering!

I tried the Mirchi bajji and Pani puri here, but unfortunately both were quite bland and lacked salt, which really took away from the flavor. They could definitely improve by adding more spice and seasoning to bring out the authentic taste. On the positive side, the person serving was very polite and friendly, and the atmosphere was pleasant. The place was clean and well maintained too.
With just a little more attention to taste, this place could be much better.

name: Idli Dosa Indian Restaurant
formatted_address: 6811 Eastman Ave, Midland, MI 48642, USA

top_reviews.text: We came here for dinner on Christmas day for our first time and were very pleased with the meal! I had the Chicken Tikka Masala and my husband had the Chettinad Chicken Curry as well as a side of the garlic naan bread. Both meals were large postpone, perfect spice level (you get to choose, we chose spicy), and everything was so fresh! Prices were reasonable and service was good. Overall a great experience, will definitely be back.

So fresh and flavorful!!! Honestly I was blown away by the Lemon Coriander soup- it's the middle of summer and it hit the spot!! It's that undercurrent of spice with the sour lemon that lays perfectly of your palette!

We had the butter chicken and Tikka masala which were perfect  with the most layered garlic naan.

And let's not forget the service these men all provided. They were swift, friendly and polite.

The restaurant is clean with a hint of humor with the full Taylor Swift album playing during our dinner.

Passing through the area on our way to Detroit this was a hidden gem we wish we could come back again
Amazing food.  So many online orders ready to go.  This spot is hot.

First time trying this restaurant! At first the condition of the restaurant had us second guessing. This was due to it honestly looking  like they were a run down place. I’m hoping to this was just due to them doing some cosmetic updating. For their previous photos from other customers, definitely have shown a difference of what the inside looked like before we came.
Now when it came to the food it was amazing!! The only issues were the following: the waiter had an hard time explaining why the chicken were three different colors lol. He didn’t really know how to explain it in words to non-Indian individuals. Next he was the only waiter working while the manager didn’t help. Which is why there was a little delay between getting appetizers, main course and finally our check. The manager was either behind the desk or no where to be seen. He should have been helping his employee out. I will give the waiter an A for his attitude when dealing with an old Karen. She was beyond rude to him and the manager only appeared at the end after the young man already handled it.

Idli Dosa catered our wedding, and they did an amazing job! The food was outstanding, and the staff were professional and attentive. Their service was exceptional, and all dishes were delicious. Guests LOVED the live nann station! In terms of communication, I would recommend confirming your event date ahead of time and asking about their timeline for items such as the invoice and other details to ensure clear communication. Overall, these experts know what they are doing and are happy to make  accommodations as needed. We would highly recommend this talented team for any event!!

Thank you, Idli Dosa!!

name: Tawa Indian Cuisine
formatted_address: 38245 W 10 Mile Rd, Farmington Hills, MI 48335, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM, 5:00 – 10:00 PM

top_reviews.text: The service was a bit slow, but the food was excellent and the staff very welcoming. The paneer lababdar was flavorful, and the garlic naan was delicious. The appetizers- Aloo bajji and Cauliflower 65, were also tasty. Must try their chikko and mango milkshake. They’re amaazing!

We ordered Andhra chicken curry with garlic naan and goat biryani. The curry and naans were delicious, and the biryani tasted great, though it was served late after a reminder.

The highlight was the unlimited dosa served with chicken curry, coconut chutney, peanut chutney, and sambar. The dosa and chicken curry combo was excellent, but the chutneys could be improved. The staff were super friendly and polite, and the ambiance was decent.

I ordered a medium spice biryani and what I got was extremely spicy completely opposite of what I asked for. It’s so hot that I can’t even eat it, my mouth is burning. I’m honestly very disappointed. I ordered because I was hungry and tired, hoping for a good meal, and this turned into a nightmare. Really frustrating experience.
Why do you guys do this?

This is one of the best mandis I’ve ever had! It reminded me of the same amazing experience I had at Desi District in Dallas. I might actually get addicted to this place — the rice and juicy meat are just wow! Every penny is totally worth it, yaar. A must-try dish for sure!

Highly recommended ❤️

I had an unforgettable dining experience at this fantastic Indian restaurant! The highlight of the meal was the mandi biryani – it was incredibly flavorful, perfectly spiced, and packed with tender, juicy meat that melted in my mouth. Every bite was a burst of aromatic spices and the rice was cooked to perfection.

name: GOLCONDA NAWAB’S INDIAN CUISINE
formatted_address: 3337 Greenfield Rd, Dearborn, MI 48120, USA

top_reviews.text: Really disappointing lunch buffet. The naan was paper thin and felt stale. I had to keep asking staff to refill multiple dishes, which shouldn’t happen when I arrived right at 12pm and the buffet runs until 2:30pm. Guests shouldn’t have to chase down basic service during peak hours. The food quality didn’t justify the $16 price at all. I won’t be back.

I recently visited Golconda Nawab and had their chili fish along with fried rice and noodles — and it was absolutely fantastic! 🤩🔥 The flavors were amazing, and honestly, I have never tasted anything like this before in Detroit. Every dish was perfectly cooked, fresh, and full of authentic taste. 🍜🐟✨
The restaurant owner was personally there, and he was very nice, polite, and welcoming. 😊👏 The customer service was excellent, which made the whole experience even more special.
Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ If you are in Detroit and love delicious food, this place is a must-try! I will definitely be coming back again! 🙌🍽️

The only redeeming part of this meal was the polite and attentive server—unfortunately, he was the lone bright spot in an otherwise dreadful experience. The butter chicken was a disaster: watery, bland, and completely devoid of flavor—more like spiced soup than a curry. The paneer dish? An unrecognizable fusion mess that tasted more like bad Chinese takeout than Indian cuisine. It’s hard to imagine how a kitchen can get both texture and taste this wrong. If you're craving real Indian food, do yourself a favor and look elsewhere.

Wow. I used to live in Dearborn so I recall various restaurants in this spot over the years. Not sure if owners have ever changed but I went back today and what a difference. The food was totally excellent. Hyderabadi chef and the chicken dum biryani was excellent, bone in and the chicken was moist, tender and not at all dry. Mirapai kodi appetizer is 10/10.! ordered it “Indian level spicy” and it was perfect. Gobi sambar rice bowl has a great flavor in the sambar, I was really happy. It’s a great comfort food.

If I could recommend anything it’s that app mirapai kodi (chicken) app “Indian spicy” level. Edit: I also ordered mirapai kodi biryani a different day since writing this, also recommend.

It’s been a long time since I was here. Will definitely be back.

The chicken photo was after I dug into it so apologies but I couldn’t hold myself back lol.

I ordered a chicken appetizer, and after taking the order, they called to say it wasn’t available. They said they would replace it with Chicken 65, but they ended up giving me something else. I even double-checked with the girl at the front desk, but it was still wrong. The food quantity was very less for the price we paid. Really disappointed with the service and quality.

name: KT's Rasoi Indian Cuisine
formatted_address: 4075 Hollywood Rd, St Joseph, MI 49085, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: The food here is absolutely incredible and the service is excellent! We’re big fans of Indian cuisine, and this place did not disappoint. We’ll definitely be back again very soon!

Ordered the Kachori Appetizer (9/10), Boneless Chicken Curry (9/10) and Paratha's (5.5/10). First time going to an Indian Restaurant that didn't have Naan bread, although this is the first time going to an Indian restaurant that DID have Paratha, Dosa, Uthapam etc etc. (Many new "to-me" things) For my own personal tastes, I would take Naan over Paratha, but everything else i tried food wise was outstanding !
Only de-merit on the star scoring was for Service. It wasn't bad, but the Restaurant was super busy with take out orders (Grubhub, Doordash, Uber Eats, Postmates etc.) so while it was quiet for inside dining, the off-site orders were slamming the kitchen. Either way, I will definitely go again because of the fantastic food !

We visited the restaurant last night, as I
was suspecting it was very good. Food is fresh, spiced just right for a non Indian who likes spicy but not blazing hot. The menu is not extensive but that is the secret, high quality food . We will be back for more. Thanks Chef

Amazing food & truly loving service! This is clearly more than a business to them, they put so much heart into it. The food was delicious & is true authentic Indian food - something that can be hard to find in the US! If you are concerned about spice, just let them know, they are very helpful. The atmosphere is comforting & light, our group loved spending time there. I wish them so much success, this is truly a gem to have in our community. There are dishes they offer that you will not find anywhere but in India, give them a try! (The mutton biryani is delicious, the meat is very tender & beautifully prepared.) Try new things, this is an amazing restaurant.

Food: 5/5 (Delicious!)
Service: 5/5 (Very kind staff, great service)
Atmosphere: 4/5 (Very clean, nice pictures)

Beautiful experience at KT's Rasoi! I love the slogan, "Good Food, Good Mood," as this is a true statement. Located in Saint Joseph, Michigan, this restaurant and the staff are inviting, down to earth, and very kind. If you never had Indian food, you're in for a treat!

The food is phenomenal! It smelled absolutely great in the restaurant! We tried the Onion Uthapam, nice onion taste, nice spice, and lovely texture; chicken and mutton dishes, very fragrant and delicious! I love the spice and the cool cucumber cream on the side. If you're not a spice person, please beware.

The service is amazing. As a new restaurant, the service out the gate was set high. I appreciate the humility, and our water cups stayed full! The menu was explained nicely, and the staff were awesome.

The atmosphere is good. Very clean and nice pictures. As time goes on, I believe the atmosphere will change as it appears they are putting their identity and touches to the new restaurant. I love traditional dishes!

Overall, it was a beautiful experience at KT's Rasoi. The food was good and I certainly left in a good mood.

name: Veg-O-Rama
formatted_address: 44930 Ford Rd, Canton, MI 48187, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text: What an interesting spot! Entirely vegan menu blending American, Indian, middle eastern, and Chinese cuisines. The theme park style interior is contrasted by the peaceful bird noises playing, but to each their own. I got the orange Chick’n dodo wrap with vegan cheese, which was great! The vegan cheese is an up-charge, just to note. I also didn’t get a ton of the orange chicken flavor, but I enjoyed it nonetheless. A fun place to check out!

I stopped in to give them a try I bought the broccoli cheese  balls,impossible burger and paneer croquette. The broccoli cheese balls and croquette was so mushy and spicy i mean all you got was SPICE couldn’t eat them and I was looking for that crunchy texture on the outside was not there. The impossible burger was ok I love the bun it was served on that’s about it .But everyone taste is different this place is something you’ll just have to try for yourself. Other than that the employee at the front counter was super friendly,helpful and nice very settle atmosphere.

Should have checked reviews before trying this place . Ambience is good and empty on a Saturday evening . Bought cauliflower wings , which are damn mushy and sour in taste . The DoDo wrap is made from store bought tortillas and is not even roasted properly . Will never go back.

If people want 5 star hotel service this place is not for you. Ouch people stay away. This place provides the best masala fries, their kathi roles are superb. Do try the new entries. They have very well maintained and clean environment. Recommend this to all desi crowd.

Highly recommended for Vegetarians.. this meets your thirst for Indian taste. One of the few Indian restaurants in Ann Arbor, Ypsi and Canton which is very close to Indian restaurants.

We ordered Masala Chai, Samosa, Masala freis 🍟, Paneer Tikka Wrap 🌯 and we got brownies as complementary. Everything was excellent. Prices are very reasonable.

Services are better than few other restaurants we tried.

Overall highly recommended

name: Madras Indian Grill
formatted_address: 2030 28th St SE, Grand Rapids, MI 49508, USA

top_reviews.text: Madras has a great lunch buffet Fri, Sat, and Sun. These are pics from Friday. An employee told us there are even MORE entrees on Saturdays and Sundays. Everything was excellent. Really GOOD flavor. My only complaint is, I’m a spice wimp and some dishes were too spicy for me. My hubs thought it was perfect (he’s not a spice wimp, but he also cannot do very spicy). Staff were very friendly and welcoming.

Madras has excellent food & and service. We loved our apps, veggie pakoras & chili paneer.
The dosa & nann were amazing. For entrees we had lamb tikka masala & the chicken Vindaloo! Two awesome dishes. We will definitely be back & we can’t wait to try out the Friday-Sunday lunch buffet!!!

We went for dinner and to start we ordered the vegetable samosas, which were rather soggy. We also had the mixed vegetable pakoras, which were ok. The lassi was delicious. I had the chicken tikka masala, it lacked some salt but fortunately it was an easy fix. The lamb was very tasty, but the meat had a lot of fat. The honey dragon chilly was good. For dessert we ordered the double ka meetha and I had to spit it...it was very unpleasant. 😳 I informed the waitress about it. The men's bathroom was dirty and the urinal was out of service. I'll end this review adding that the servers were kind.

While we came for dinner they do offer a lunch buffet. Our dinner was over great, food met all our expectations and had good flavors, serving sizes and aroma. Service was ok, a little behind but with 5 tables and 1 worker it can be that way, maybe it was just that time of the evening. The inside still reminds me of the seafood place it was before as nothing really has changed it seems, but thats not why you come here. The food did seem very authentic and fresh. Bathrooms were ok.

So yummy!   I can’t wait to go back- this is my favorite place around this area for Indian food. I brought my mom here- it was her first time eating Indian food and she is HOOKED.   The spice is perfection. So many delicious menu choices!!!!!  I want to come back for the buffet so I can try a bunch of different things, my taste buds LOVE it here.
Friendly service. The space is clean and open.

name: Mithu | Srilankan & Indian Cuisine
formatted_address: 4309 Kalamazoo Ave SE, Grand Rapids, MI 49508, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 4:00 – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 1:00 – 8:30 PM | Friday: 1:00 – 8:30 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 8:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM

top_reviews.text: My daughter and I really enjoy Indian food, and after reading the online reviews decided to drive up from Holland to have lunch. The staff was very friendly. My daughter had the Butter Chicken and said it was her favorite of the places she's had it from. We also order and appetizer, butter naan, and it had a dish I cant remember the name. I'm sorry about that, but it was very good. After eatingasmuch as we could my daughter said we are definitely coming back.

This was a cathartic experience.

I walk in—boom.

So many smells. I’m overwhelmed in the best way. In my daze I focus on an empty corner booth to my right. As I make my way over, I pass a couple praying, another couple on a date, and I catch snippets of borderline-taboo conversation from a nearby table. My first thought: the people-watching tonight is about to be a five out of five.

I sit, and the gentleman serving the dining area comes over immediately and greets me with, “My friend, how are you?”
We’ve never met. But clearly, we are friends now.

In a hushed tone, I share the first secret of this new friendship: it’s my first time eating Indian food. He smiles, and together we choose the safe options.

The order:
Buttered naan
Chicken tikka
White rice

Ten minutes later, I share the second secret—just as hushed as the first: “I like spice.”

Only then do I look around and realize that everyone already eating is coughing, wiping sweat from their foreheads, and occasionally taking a break to recover. Oh no, what have I done? Can I handle “spice” real spice. Their spice?

Life has taught me one thing: it gets more exciting when you ignore its little red flags. I’ve never backed down before, and I’m not starting now.
Too late for second thoughts—give me all the spice.

My plate arrives steaming—sizzling.
The naan? Perfection.
My friend places the rice down gently, then slides a small cup of spices across the table. “Your spices,” he says.

The rest goes by in a blur. My meal is soundtracked by waves of taboo conversation, the soft prayers from earlier, and a late-evening family debate where a young Indian woman tries to convince her parents to accept her boyfriend—who quietly stares at the table and eats.
The people-watching? A six out of five.

It felt like the entire world was represented in this one dining room—a collective thumbs-up from all cultures and walks of life.

Amazing food. Amazing service.
And to my new friend: I’ll see you soon.

It was wonderful to enjoy some authentic Sri Lankan dishes after a while. I tried the Egg Kottu, soya meat curry (vegetarian) and masala tea. It was flavorful and delicious! The atmosphere was inviting, the service was great, and the food was excellent! Highly recommend this spot to anyone looking for Sri Lankan food in the area.

Seriously good. We had the egg curry, and chicken tikka masala. Both were the best we have had anywhere in town.

Garlic naan was on point.

Good portion sizes. We took half home for later.

Buffet - It appears they no longer have it if you were looking for that info like we were before visiting.

Medium heat level was plenty hot for me. I can tolerate decent amount of heat but prefer 5/10.  Asking for medium got me a 6/10 so pretty on par.

We will be back minimum once a month. Just wow.. cant believe I’ve gone this long without trying this place.

I saw the 4.9 review rating and decided to give it a try. SO glad that we did! Everything we tried was delicious.  I got the Beef Curry and my girlfriend got Chicken tika masala .We also got the Garlic butter naan bread. The naan bread went great with both of our dishes. They didn't have the Lassi I really wanted to try unfortunately , but other than that everything was awesome. 10/10 would recommend and we will be returning.

name: Yoga Ganesha Cultural Center
formatted_address: 4951 Rochester Rd, Troy, MI 48085, USA
hours: Monday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM

name: Namaste Yoga Center
formatted_address: 3121 Rochester Rd, Royal Oak, MI 48073, USA
hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 8:45 AM – 12:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 – 11:30 AM | Sunday: 10:30 – 11:30 AM

top_reviews.text: I joined Trishna's June prenatal yoga session and had a fantastic time. As a new mom herself, she's very understanding of both the physical and mental needs of her students. Her class is relaxing and felt great on my 3rd trimester body! Highly recommend to any expectant mamas.

I'm a teacher at Namaste but I'm also a student. I began practicing at Namaste over 12 years ago and consider it my yoga home. I felt drawn to the studio for the building's historic charm and big, oversized windows. I returned for the array of kind and generous teachers and staff, all as welcoming as the next. I remember wanting to study yoga but didn't know where to start or felt like I didn't belong at certain studios. When you come in, you'll just know it's right.
I've been 5 or 6 times now and it's been great each time! Small, cozy space with a variety of times and teachers.
Welcoming community  for all skill levels with a great variety & wonderful instructors.  Have been happy with every class I have attended!!! My favorite class is the Somatic Yoga one on Tuesdays!!!!

Full disclosure, I did my yoga teacher training at Namaste and I teach on the schedule regularly. That being said, I can share my experience as a student for in-person and virtual classes and the reiki services offered by Rachele Rose.

THE STUDIO: Safe parking lot with ample spaces, two private bathrooms/ changing rooms, comfortable entrance/lobby area, beautiful studio space to practice in, tons of props

REIKI: I highly recommend Rachele Rose. She provided me with a session that helped me find clarity and wholeness. The best way I can describe it is that it was like peeling off a layer of armor, which let more love and clarity in. The music and scents were delightful and Rachele was very professional and skilled at her craft.

CLASSES: I love to visit the studio when I can, and there are virtual offerings too for those days when you don't want to leave the house. I like that it isn't a "hot yoga" studio. Most of the classes are hatha-based, though there is a good variety on the schedule.

name: Lotus Ayurveda Wellness Center
formatted_address: 34593 Grand River Ave, Farmington, MI 48335, USA
hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Sunday: Closed

top_reviews.text: My experience with Dr Raj is that he gives u his full compassionate attention to help you with your health.  His treatment is personalized and based on a great expertise in the body, mind, and soul, finding root causes and healing from within.  This kind of care is unmatched.  The freedom that is realized by taking control of your life by shifting food, movement, sleep or even mind patterns is real therapy.   If you are ready to learn how to be involved with your health, I recommend you meet with Dr. Raj.

My family members and I have experienced firsthand improvements in our health under Dr. Raj’s care. After trying traditional medical options with little to no results—losing valuable time, effort, and money—we finally found meaningful and lasting improvement through Dr. Raj’s expertise.

His approach is gentle, natural, and incredibly effective. With no side effects, he guided us toward simple yet powerful lifestyle changes, particularly around food choices and daily healthy practices. These changes may seem small, but their impact has been truly transformative.

Dr. Raj introduced me to the philosophy of “Let food be thy medicine,” and it has completely changed the way I approach my health. I am forever grateful for his years of knowledge, wisdom, and experience.

I wholeheartedly recommend Dr. Raj to anyone seeking natural, holistic healing. If you are looking for a gentle and effective path to better health, at least give him a try—you won’t regret it.

I had a great experience at Doctor Raj’s Ayurvedic shop! He was incredibly friendly, knowledgeable, and helpful in explaining the products and their benefits. The store has a great selection of authentic Ayurvedic items, and his genuine care for customers really stands out. I highly recommend visiting the store
Especially the chai was so healthy must try it

For me, Ayurveda is the truest form of health care, and fortunately, Raj knows his stuff! Every time I've met with him, it seems like he's tapped into information that's divinely tailored just for me. The advice he shares, the stories he tells, all end up resonating deeply and become building blocks that stabilize my foundation. I'm looking forward to sharing more about my progress in the future. ☯️

At Lotus Ayurveda I feel it is a true healing place and Dr. Raj gives a lot from his heart & spirit, it’s not just remedies as pills or therapies he offer. I feel blessed to meet him and helping to my health and my journey. Thank You Dr. Raj.

name: Arogya Veda
formatted_address: 2458 N Dixboro Rd, Ann Arbor, MI 48105, USA

top_reviews.text: I wholeheartedly recommend Dr. Deepa! Her knowledge, care, and dedication were truly outstanding in addressing a health concern. I was met with understanding and a personalized Ayurvedic treatment plan that led to remarkable healing. I am incredibly grateful for the positive impact on my well-being. If you're looking for a skilled and compassionate Ayurvedic practitioner who delivers real results, look no further. Five stars! I will continue seeing her for other health benefits. Thanks Dr. Deepa, we are truly grateful!

name: Wat Dhammasala Forest Monastery
formatted_address: 14780 Beardslee Rd, Perry, MI 48872, USA
hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM

top_reviews.text: A true refuge of sanity within a chaotic world.  This is the REAL DEAL: an internationally-certified monastery and temple with lineage tracing back to the greatest Theravada monks of the last two centuries (not a joke: the Abbot of Wat Dhammasala trained under Maha Boowa in Thailand).  I've been involved with the monastery since the second week following the arrival of Ajahn Khemasanto (the Abbot); when we all gathered in a tiny University Village apartment to hear his Dhamma talks.  I've seen it move locations multiple times and grow across decades (and I served on the Board of Directors for many years) until at least it arrived at its current location - with the stunning, expansive, and tranquil acreage in Perry.  BOTTOM LINE: If your desire is to experience the authentic, actual teachings of the Buddha (i.e., Dhamma) - and not the silly, touchy-feely, watered-down, new-agey nonsense that passes for much of "Buddhism" in the U.S. (e.g., "we're all connected as one.." etc. etc.) - this is the place to visit and learn.  You will be part of a tradition that traces directly back to the forest monasteries of Thailand, and through history back to the teachings of the Buddha more than 2,600 years ago.  Enough said.  PS  Addressing the negative reviews below: the monks won't necessarily respond favorably to all who visit, as they have no desire to "recruit" or "convert."  As with all forest monasteries, their responsiveness toward visitors depends entirely upon the perceived motives compelling the visit.  Those who obviously are there only to argue or bait or disprove typically are ignored or dismissed; as the monks have no interest in such intrigues.  And with regard to the post below about the original teachings, they were in Pali, not Sanskrit.  :-)

The American monk has WAYYY too much ego. He arrogantly claims that Thervada monks are 'The keepers of Buddhism'. The other monk who stated that Zen Buddhism has  a 'Fat Buddha' is incorrect, as well. Hotei is a Chinese deity, NOT Japanese. Siddhartha Gautama was Nepalese, not Chinese, not Thai and not Japanese. They probably think that the Buddha's teachings were written in Thai instead of Sanskrit, originally.
Wow!  Thank you for being so welcoming. I came out here alone to walk a few times.  It is beautiful.  Then I came yesterday to an event and was welcomed with open arms by the community.  🙏

Great place to go if you want to learn about Buddhism they teach Thai Buddhism which is slightly different than most people in America think the monks have been ordained in Thailand the head monk is a great person we'll talk with you and answer any question you have if you are a beginner to Buddhism I would highly recommend starting here and learn the traditional Buddhism teachings
It is a Thai temple. Monks are American who are able to explain Buddhism in English.  Every Saturday the Monk talk is held. Great peaceful place to go.

Neelam and her students are amazing!  They did a Bollywood dance workshop for my Honors Class at UM-Flint, which focuses on the Indian Ocean world.  She showed us clips of classic and more recent Bollywood films, used them to talk about portrayals of love and marriage in these films (one of the topics of my class), and interspersed this with opportunities for us to try several of the dances in these and other films.  I had students I totally didn't expect coming out of themselves and dancing their hearts out, and some of the students asked Neelam if she would ever be able to do a dance workshop for a birthday party of other social event.

In short, this was everything I had hoped for and so much more. I will definitely have Neelam back to my class, and I highly recommend her to anyone interested in the dance traditions of South Asia, Bollywood, or related topics!

Neelam is so welcoming and loves to dance and teach. She makes it fun and we learn about her culture too. Top ratings and top recommendations for this wonderful lady. Thank you for sharing Bollywood and traditional dances with us!

top_reviews.text:
DJ Naveen was absolutely incredible! He kept the energy high from start to finish and knew exactly how to read the crowd. His music selection was perfect, the transitions were smooth, and he made sure everyone was on the dance floor the entire night.

What really stood out was his professionalism and friendly attitude—he took all our requests, understood the vibe we wanted, and delivered beyond expectations. Our guests are still talking about how amazing the music was!

If you want your event to be unforgettable, DJ Naveen is the one to book! Highly recommended!

DJ Naveen helped us make our dream weekend come true. He was very professional throughout the planning process, and he brought so many great ideas to the table. His lighting really set the mood for all of our events and made the room look phenomenal. He had the music going throughout the baraat and reception and we had such a great time partying with him. Even though I (the groom) was long distance, Naveen made the extra effort to set up calls to help plan for the weekend. DJ Naveen was very professional, organized, and easy to work with and I would recommend him for any of your events. He added so many extra touches for us such as getting my wife a spotlight for her bridal entrance, organizing a truck for my baraat, setting up an awesome photo booth for our reception, and throwing out glow sticks during our reception! All those little details made our event so much more memorable. Naveen was also a great MC and helped keep the crowd excited and the program moving. Naveen can make your wedding a dream wedding like he did for us!

DJ Naveen is a pleasure to work with and made our wedding dream come true. He is a professional and has an infectious energy. My husband and I didn’t get enough time to decide the entire playlist so we gave him a few songs to play and decided to trust Naveen to take care of the rest! He played all the songs we requested but we are so glad to trust him to select the music for the rest of the event. I asked him to play an instrumental piece of his choice for the entrance to my wedding ceremony. He played the most beautiful music and I got so many compliments on how perfect it was! We had a blast at our reception party. He played the perfect songs and the dance floor was never empty! We highly recommend DJ Naveen!

Naveen brings experience and professionalism to the DJ booth that is immediately apparent. He was always easy to work with, accommodated requests and changes to the plan seamlessly, and overall reduced stress for us.

He's excellent as an MC doing introductions, setting the tone for our night, and giving us and our families a memorable walkout.

Naveen does a good job to read the room and adjust the music to match. He didn't force a song in just because it was on our list, if it was clearly not going to play well with the people dancing. Keeping both sides happy in a mixed culture wedding is challenging, but it didn't feel that way during our reception.

Also, his up lighting, videography, photo booth were all excellent. Lighting set the mood properly, photo booth worked seamlessly, and he shared videos that we didn't even ask for or know we were getting -- a pleasant surprise.

I'd recommend him to anybody, regardless of culture.

We reached out to Naveen to be a DJ for our destination wedding and I'm so glad we did. Not only was he our DJ, he was also our MC and he did a great job energizing the crowd. Due to it being a fusion wedding and we were having fusion ceremonies, not all our guests were familiar with the traditions of select events. DJ Naveen did a great job at explaining the rituals and purposes of each event. Guests felt like they were well informed. Fantastic DJ who loves what he does.

top_reviews.text: Nicole and Emerald City Designs made my wedding dream vision come true! I wasn’t quite sure exactly what I wanted besides lots of candles, neutrals and a romantic vibe and Nicole helped me bring that to life. From the first meeting I was very impressed at the level of professionalism, experience and options Emerald City Designs has to offer. The day of my wedding, I didn’t have to worry about a thing (like seriously not a thing), as ECD had everything set up as well as taken down so seamlessly. I couldn’t have asked for a better experience and myself and my guests were blown away at how beautiful everything looked. This entire service is extremely reasonably priced for the amazing quality and service you receive and I would 10000% recommend using ECD for your wedding or any event.

We photograph a lot of weddings at some of Detroit's best venues.  It is always reassuring when we see a team of workers from Emerald City Designs setting up the decor for the day.  They take pride and care in each and every one of their weddings and clients.  To be considered one of Detroit's best, you know they must be good.  Whether it is for a wedding, baby shower, or corporate event, Emerald City has you covered.

Loved our experience with Emerald City Designs in planning our daughter’s wedding. They made all of our ideas come to life in a beautiful way. The wedding coordinator kept everything running smoothly on the wedding day and allowed the wedding party and our families to just enjoy the day without any concerns. The floral and table designs as well as the stationary items were all beautifully done. Highly recommend working with ECD!

We are so grateful to Emerald City Flowers for bringing our moody, charming November wedding vision to life. The florals were absolutely breathtaking — truly beyond anything I could have imagined. I received endless compliments from our guests about how stunning everything looked. From the bouquets to the menus, table settings, candles, and even our seating chart, every detail was perfect and so thoughtfully designed.

Nicole was an absolute dream to work with. She is warm, kind, full of life, and incredibly talented. I felt so cared for throughout the entire planning process. She made everything stress-free, enjoyable, and truly fun. I adored my bridal bouquet, my bridesmaids’ bouquets, and every floral element she created. It all came together so beautifully and made the day feel magical.

Thank you, Nicole, and the entire Emerald City Flowers team, for giving us our dream wedding florals. We loved everything — truly, everything.

With love,
Jaclyn and Stefan Withrow

Hiring Emerald City Designs was one of the best decisions we made for our wedding. They provided design services for our florals, rentals (linens, chairs, photo wall), and stationary. Their process makes planning so easy and the design meeting was the most fun we had during wedding planning. We worked with Nicole and the way she brought our vision to life was incredible. The team at ECD is so professional and efficient. We were fortunate that ECD works closely with our venue so they handled all of the logistics and details, taking that added stress off our plates and on the wedding day, the set-up and breakdown was seamless. Thank you ECD for being a part of our big day! (Photos in review by: Breanne Rochelle Photography)

top_reviews.text: A little bit of a different take here; I am a wedding photographer and have worked with all kinds of vendors over the past 17 years of doing business. I will be recommending her to my couples!

THE BEST TO EVER DO IT!!
I cannot recommend the Clink and Co team enough. Not only did they make sure our wedding day was flawless, but they truly feel like friends we gained throughout the process.
Jamie was incredible and helped so much in the last few months leading up to the wedding and made the day of perfect. I had absolutely no worries because of her. Christina was amazing at bringing our design and decor to life, and we had so much fun working with her along the way. Corinna brought me so much peace and comfort on our wedding day. It meant everything.
I truly cannot thank or recommend this team enough. Our wedding day came together so beautifully because of the Clink and Co team.

I don’t even know where to start tbh. From completely engraving herself into our celebration to making sure every last minute freak out was handled peacefully she is probably the best woman for the job. It was never “we can’t do this” and always “let me make a few calls”. Culturally, we let doves free into the sky after walking out of church and of course I didn’t remember about that until the morning of the wedding. She literally got us situated perfectly. This was the same for every little thing that would come up. She also had the smallest window of time to work with us. Christina did it all with a beautiful smile on her face. I miss talking to her on daily basis :( if you think you don’t need a wedding planner thing again and give her a call 💝 ( also very important, trust her vendors; whoever she recommended were amazing people to have by you on your wedding day)

If you’re looking for the dream team to bring your wedding vision to life, look no further than Christina and Corinna at Clink & Co. Designs. These two are the definition of real-life fairy godmothers. Their attention to detail is unmatched, and they handled every little thing that popped up throughout the day before I even had time to worry about it.

They brought such professionalism, warmth, and years of experience into every moment of our wedding. The entire day flowed more smoothly than I ever could have imagined, and that is entirely thanks to them. From timeline management to décor to last-minute needs, they took care of everything with grace and ease.

Trust me—you need to book them. Christina and Corinna made our wedding day absolutely amazing, and I’m forever grateful for the magic they created. Highly, highly recommend!

Jamie, Hannah and the Clink & Co team were absolutely incredible! Christina was my first point of contact when I initially reached out for services, and she was a pleasure to work with from the very beginning. They made the entire process so smooth by helping me confirm all my vendors ahead of time and finalizing my day of timeline. Overall, they didn’t miss a single detail, went out of their way to check on me, the groom, wedding party, and our guests, and set up our décor beautifully. I would 100000% recommend Clink & Co! Thank you for everything!

name: Indian Springs Metropark
formatted_address: 5200 Indian Trail, White Lake, MI 48386, USA
hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM

top_reviews.text: Beautiful park, has lots of things to do. Nature trails, discovery center, park, paved hike and bike trails. Gorgeous views of wildlife and nature. Hosts events, has a beautiful banquet center overlooking the pond.

We were so excited to make it here! It’s so fascinating that this is where the headwaters of the Huron River originate. Really it’s a bit further north at Big Lake, but the stretch of the Huron through Indian Springs is really neat.

Having kayaked the full length of the Huron (from just south of Commerce Lake to Lake Erie), we were stoked to see this part of the region. It did not disappoint! We visited near the end of April. Several of the floating bridges over the Huron proper were impassable due to high water. No matter- it was still a blast!

We saw so many skunk cabbage as well as spring ephemerals like Mayapples and trillium! We saw waterfowl and various birds.

We will definitely be back at different times of year to see how it changes!

Well worth the $10 to support this well run park!

They have a new cool playground rly jig with lots of different things for the kids to try according their age. Paid $10 for a day pass.

This Metro park is huge, has lots of good trails for walking, biking running, etc.
There’s a pretty nice splash pad with lots of picnic tables around and a playground nearby.

There are no restrooms or changing rooms close to the splash pad area or water fountains which is a bummer, only mobile restrooms very very dirty…🥲

There’s some other fun areas like the climbing wall and the big rope spiderwebs!

I loved the Nature Center the building itself is gorgeous you can go to the lowest floor go inside the dome underwater, pretty cool. Other than that there’s not a lot to see inside.

So many great options for hiking/walking. Paved paths, gravel, grassy; in woods, meadows and past ponds. Very well maintained! I love it all year round, but especially in late spring early summer with all the blooms!

Visited the park on thanksgiving day, wanted to get a nice and long walk before the thanksgiving feast!

Was really glad I visited, park was almost empty just few visitors, I did enjoy reading facts about birds and trees along the hike, I did enjoy the scenery in winter so I can imagine it’s stunning in spring, i highly recommend visiting as this park is one of the hidden gems in MI

top_reviews.text: Was amazing and so beautiful.  Asked or an urn arrangement for my mother's memorial party and it was SO BEAUTIFUL. top_reviews.text: Going to this clinic overall was a great experience.  My only negative was when I was seen by Dr. Z. I was told to stop making myself cough.  Well, ASTHMA when a treatment is NEEDED doesn't always agree with that. I ultimately went elsewhere, got my treatment and two inhalers and a prescription cough suppressant and another medication.  Over the course of a few days the phlegm,  wheezing and cough went away. She also implied that I was a smoker. Not at all. Don't  drink either. Don't live with a smoker. Doctors aren't perfect, I get that. She simply and plainly dropped the ball. I'm not real fond of the students/interns being in the room with the doctor.  Three, four people at once with eyes locked on me while I answer the same questions for the second or third time is a bit uncomfortable.  It comes with the territory.  Just voicing how I feel about it. Oh, btw, if you must have them use the Woodward pharmacy,  inhalers are not covered there (not free).
