name: Pravin P Puri M.D.
formatted_address: 1080 Kirts Blvd #700, Troy, MI 48084, USA
hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed

top_reviews.text:
I want to share my honest experience with Dr. Puri so anyone considering abdominoplasty can have a clearer idea of what to expect. I had my surgery in mid-August, including an abdominoplasty and liposuction on both sides of my hips.

Before choosing this doctor, I consulted with several other doctors, but a friend recommended him. My first consultation went smoothly—he explained the surgery and said I would end up in good shape. He assured me that my stomach would be flat and both hips would be treated with liposuction. Based on that, I scheduled my surgery three weeks later.

The surgery itself went smoothly and was performed cleanly. However, after the surgery, I was surprised that the clinic did not follow up with me—no calls or texts to check on my recovery or see if I had any issues. The doctor told me that if I had any questions, I could call the office. When I did call, the nurse responded to answer my questions, but she was not patient or kind at all. Even when she tried to smile, it was clear from her face that she was angry or annoyed.

Then, I had an appointment one week later; the doctor looked at me and said everything was fine.

My main issues came afterward:

My skin is not tight enough.

The liposuction on my hips was minimal, and I still have fat there.

My upper stomach was not treated at all, even though during the first consultation, I was told it would be.

I had been saving for years for this surgery, so it was very disappointing not to get the results I hoped for. I am not happy with the result. I asked the doctor about my loose skin, and he said that this is as tight as it gets. Even three months after surgery, my stomach looks as though I am pregnant because the upper tummy was not addressed.

Additionally, I found communication frustrating. When I raised concerns about areas that were not treated or results I wasn’t happy with, the doctor would either ignore my questions or avoid giving clear answers. He told me that he had done liposuction in my lower part, but in reality, he did not. If he had actually done liposuction in that area, you would notice bruising or skin discoloration in the treated area, but there was none—he only placed the two tubes. He might have thought we wouldn’t know, but this is 2025; with social media, there are millions of doctors explaining the full process and what should be done for this type of surgery. This made me feel that he was not honest, and I regret choosing him for my surgery.

If you are considering going to this doctor, keep in mind that you may not get the results you want. I even told my friend, who was thinking of going to him because I had surgery with him, exactly what she can expect to get and what she will not get  it’s ultimately your choice.

I’m grateful to Dr. Pravin Puri and his entire team for the exceptional care I received. I had several lipomas and moles removed, and from the very first consultation, Dr. Puri stood out for his professionalism, clear communication, and genuine compassion.
He took the time to explain everything thoroughly, making sure I understood the procedures and what to expect.
His surgical skill is outstanding—my recovery was smooth, and the results exceeded my expectations.
Dr. Puri’s staff was equally kind, supportive, and always ready to help.
They made the entire experience as stress-free and comfortable as possible.
I wholeheartedly recommend Dr. Puri to anyone seeking a skilled and compassionate plastic surgeon.
I couldn’t be more thankful for the care I received.

I had an amazing experience with Dr. Puri and their team. From my initial consultation to the post-surgery follow-ups, the entire process was smooth and professional. Dr. Puri took the time to understand my goals, answered all of my questions, and made me feel comfortable and confident in my decision. The results were beyond my expectations, and I am so happy with the outcome. The staff was always friendly, and the office was clean and welcoming. I highly recommend Dr. Puri to anyone considering cosmetic surgery – they truly provide outstanding care and expertise!

I was familiar with this practice due to 3 of my sisters experiences.  When I needed a bilateral masectomy with reconstruction, I didn't need to look elsewhere. Even my doctors advised me I had the best. Everyone in the office should be recognized for how friendly and professional they make it. Dr. Puri always made me feel confident of the end results.

Dr. Puri and all of the staff in the office made my first plastic surgery experience a spectacular and out-of-this-world occasion! I was so ready to finally see a change in myself and he made it possible. The consultations were so easy and the office staff made the insurance process way less nerve-wracking than I thought it would be. The actual surgery day and post-op period were far less painful than I thought they would be. Dr. Puri had great bedside manner and was so reassuring. I'm ecstatic with the results and am so glad I chose this office. I'd absolutely recommend them for any plastic surgery experience.

hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: 4:00 – 7:00 PM

top_reviews.text: A big temple nicely maintained. We enjoyed Durga puja, whenever we are on Michigan. All members with their families join hands for the celebration.  All can offer floral Anjali during puja and have Prasad by everyone.

"Marked Durga Ashtami with a soulful visit to the temple, surrounded by devotion and festivity."
Thank you Durga temple team members for this devotional service.🙏

Durga temple is our go-to place for any religious activities. There is always something going on every weekend and people from many states join the temple for their puja. The biggest puja the temple has is Durga puja. There is also Kali puja, Sri Krishna Jonmashthomi, Bangla Pohela Boishakh (new year) and many other celebrations. Everyone loves the temple and does whatever they can to help out with anything. If you are ever in the area, please visit the temple. Best time to visit is Sunday.
Durga temple in Michigan. Big temple with parking lot. Good for children too. Children can play.

Even though the surrounding is not well developed, this Temple serves the devotees very well. Great hospitality. Parking is a problem during the Durga Puja time but on other days, it is very easy access to the temple from the parking lot

top_reviews.text: Awesome value, great people. Food was amazing definitely putting it in my rotation of Indian food. Garlic cheese nan out of this world. Everything was great.

If you’re looking for amazing Indian food in the Chesterfield area, this place is a must-try! Hands down the best Indian food I’ve had around here. Everything we ordered was absolutely delicious — rich, flavorful sauces, perfectly cooked rice, and fresh naan. You can tell everything is made with care and authentic spices. The butter chicken was especially a standout!

The service was also excellent and the atmosphere was warm and welcoming. Check out the photo of our meal — it tasted even better than it looks! Definitely coming back soon.

This is definitely a 10/10 when it comes to the food, service and atmosphere. The staff always pleasant, it’s clean, Food DELICIOUS. My favorite  Indian cuisine. Get enough food and TRUST me you will want more. Chicken Tikka Masala, Butter chicken and Lamb Biryani🫶🏽❤️ The Best!! Thanks for your amazing food and service

Bar none the best Indian cuisine in the area. Every dish I have tried here has been great, and the service fantastic. Ajanta is so nice and always greets us with a smile. Make sure to try the samosas and the butter chicken. Highly recommend.

This was our first visit to this location...and I couldn't tell you how thrilled I was that there's now one so close to home! The owner Ajanta greeted us and also waited on us tonight with such a welcoming and warm smile. She was absolutely the nicest and friendliest lady I've encountered in a long time.  The food was Fantastic and atmosphere gave us an "at home" feeling! So if you enjoy authentic Indian cuisine,  I highly recommend you check her out! Looking forward to many more visits!

name: Curry House Indian & Nepali cuisine
formatted_address: 930 Trowbridge Rd, East Lansing, MI 48823, USA

top_reviews.text: Best Indian food I’ve had in East Lansing. Food was out of this world flavorful and good. I was blown away by the biryani, incredible flavors. Everything felt very authentic. Butter chicken was also amazing, and the garlic naan was made with real garlic. Service was kind and atmosphere was homey and comfortable. I will be coming back often!

By far the best Indian restaurant we’ve been to in EL. I tried chicken momo which was delicious! The dipping sauce was perfectly sweet. The Onion Pakoda was flavorful and crispy with a nice curried center. Highly recommend! The place fills up fast so get here early!

Early dinner on Sunday. We ordered vegetable momos in chili sauce, Aloo Gobi, and a naan. all the food came out hot and was excellent.  Servers were friendly, attentive, and prompt.  Restaurant was clean and well lit; utilitarian but a nice space.
I've been to the lunch buffets as well, both times the food selection and tastes were excellent.  Highly recommend going.

Food was excellent. I got the medium spice and it was the perfect level of spicy. I had Garlic Naan, Chiefs Special Chicken Biryani, and my husband had the Chicken Curry. Everything was phenomenal! Really good service, and they had high chairs for babies.

Rating this as a 4 Star, since there is always room for improvements.

I ordered the Fried Chicken Momo(Mild), garlic Naan, Goat Cury (Mild), Pista Kulfi, and a Masala Chai through grub hub.
The Pros: Amazing flavors!
The fried chicken momos were very juicy and tender while still having some crunch.
The Garlic Naan is pillowy with great fresh garlicy butter flavor.
Goat Curry had was so flavorful! the meat was fall apart tender.
Pista Kulfi - YUMMM! I would eat this all the time if I could. Nutty and creamy.
*I also ordered way too much food for my self but the prices are very reasonable! Especially coming from a delivery service!

The Cons:
Masala Chai... I unfortunately did not drink this. Beverage arrived without any tamper seals and splashed all over. Since order was through a delivery service, I am uncomfortable drinking this since I can not tell if it has been tampered with or just spilled.

IT SMELLED AMAZING THO! I will for sure be making a trip over to try it one of these days.

Goat Curry: (photo below) Small splinters of bone, this is more of a caution than a con. Bones = more flavor, so I understand the use of them in the curry, but obviously small bone splinters can be dangerous!

top_reviews.text: This appears to be a small family owned business serving up food native to India and surrounding areas. Atmosphere is quite basic. Party tried the butter chicken, biryani, and naan. All were prepared well/ flavorful and it was reasonably priced for the portions.

We did not have high expectations given the fact it was empty and small, but HOLY COW the food was amazing. We had the samosas and tikka masala. Incredible flavor, perfect texture. The food couldn’t have been better. We will be back. I would have taken a photo of the food before we ate, but we were starving and it smelled amazing.
Absolutely delicious! We ordered the samosas chat, garlic naan, chicken tikka masala, and lamb Rogan Josh. All excellent, great flavors. We will be back for sure.

The only reason I am rating a 2* is because this place isn't what it was for the last so many years. This was a place that my husband and I would go when he took a day off from work so we could have a nice date alone together. We always ordered the same thing. Now the appetizer is different. My favorite is no longer on the menu, and his favorite it tasted like they added way more sugar too or however they got it that sweet. They let me order my favorite dish but it was overly sweet and that's not how the dish is supposed to be either. The only item that was the same was the garlic Naan.

Not sure if there was an owner switch but if so it wasn't for the best and we unfortunately have to find somewhere else now.
Our dinner was very yummy! We tried it for the first time tonight and the staff was very sweet and attentive. The chicken korma and coconut tofu curry was amazing and we also loved the garlic naan!

name: Halal Eastside Eats & Market
formatted_address: 11935 E Warren Ave, Detroit, MI 48213, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM

top_reviews.text:
I ordered the lamb ribs with mashed potatoes and vegetables, and they were amazing. The ribs were incredibly tender, soft, and fell right off the bone. They were full of flavor and cooked perfectly. We also tried the chicken Alfredo, which was super creamy and well seasoned without being heavy. The Caesar salad was fresh and crisp, which was a nice balance alongside the pasta.

We also ordered the chef’s burger with seasoned fries. The caramelized onions added so much flavor that I didn’t even feel like I needed a sauce, which says a lot because I usually do. For dessert, the carrot cake was delicious, especially the frosting.

A really nice touch was that they had Salaam Cola, which is always great to see at a halal restaurant. They also have a small market area with fridges for drinks and ice cream, plus shelves with sauces, beans, and other mini grocery items. It felt welcoming and convenient. The owners seemed like nice people too. Overall, great food and a great experience.

Paid almost $40 for a rack of lamb ribs that tasted extremely gamey and had 2x the amount of fat than meat. And the Mac n cheese tastes straight out of a box. Plus all that money for food they couldn’t even provide a fork. Looks good but don’t taste as such.

After eating a few pieces I decided to rinse the bbq off the meat to see what the pieces actually look like. 75% fat and 25% meat.

Im visiting from California.  Came in around 1145pm drove 25 mins based off their Google reviews and pictures. Amd Halal Eastside Eats &market did not disappoint.  I got a custom order that came out on a tray. A mix if you will ,consisting of a lobster tail and 2 lamb chops ,4 lamb ribs,  rice ,veggies and tika masala with a side of butter sauce to dip the tika in. Sauces were tzaziki, hot honey , bang bang ( which i didn't care for. But everything else was above good to 🔥 fire. Ask them about adding a lobster tail for $22. And or do a custom tray. This ended up being about 60 plus tax.  A small business that woukd love the opportunity to serve you. Come down, put down the fast food and come here and try something fresh and good. Prices are fair for what you get..its not cheap, but you dont wanna eat cheap..cheap usually isn't good. American food, Indian food and over 30 years in the kitchen.

I ordered the lamb chop dinner on DoorDash and it was incredible! I ordered them medium rare and they were not overcooked.  They were tender, flavorful, and the price was great for what you get.  Loved the mashed potatoes and veggies that came with the chops.  My husband got the beef patties and samosas, which were delicious and came with a tasty sauce.  Consider me a regular! The food was fire.

As late-night visitors arriving from Toronto, we were absolutely craving a satisfying meal, and Halal Eastside Eats & Market was our first stop in Detroit. We are so glad we found this spot.
​We ordered Cheese Pizza and the Eastside Meat Pizza, and I can confidently say neither disappointed. The Cheese Pizza was perfectly prepared, hot, gooey, and deeply satisfying. The Eastside Meat Pizza was the true standout loaded with Halal toppings. If you're looking for flavorful Halal pizza, this is your destination.
​Beyond the incredible food, the service was truly excellent. A huge shoutout to Mahi, who provided exceptional, friendly service and gave us great recommendations. His warmth and attention instantly made us feel welcome.
​If you are visiting Detroit and find yourself craving halal pizza, you absolutely need to check out Halal Eastside Eats & Market. While we only tried the pizza, they have a wide range of items on the menu and based on what we tasted, we assume the rest of the menu is equally flavorful.

name: Halal Pit Stop
formatted_address: 3951 E 10 Mile Rd, Warren, MI 48091, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

top_reviews.text:
From the smash burger 🍔 to the onion rings 🧅 to the wing dings 🍗 and the jerk chicken rice platter… this is hands down some of the best food I’ve had in years.

I used to come here all the time growing up, so imagine my surprise when I found out a guy who watched over me since I was a youngin’ — he and his family actually own the restaurant now. That made the experience even better.

And let me tell you… the food is amaaaaaazing. Especially if you love spicy food 🌶️🔥.

Right now I’m stuffed like a pig 😅 but every bite was worth it. If you’re ever in the area, this place is definitely a must-try.

This place is amazing!  The customer service was great and the food even better.  The mixed rice was amazing.  Never had anything like it but  I will be back for more.  Also had a triple burger.  It was fantastic as well.  The drift sauce is out of this world.  The onion rings are seasoned unlike most places.  Cooked perfectly and the onions almost melt in your mouth!  Definetly worth the short wait.  15 mins and I had fresh hot food with no complaints.

I have this restaurant every weekend and it is now part of my routine. They added new menu items and my favorite is either the jerk chicken over rice or the jerk chicken wrap. I would go as far as saying the jerk chicken over rice is probably the best tasting food I have had from restaurants in a 50 mile radius of here. The burgers are a perfect combo of price for portion. The drift sauce and other ingredients being used to make the burgers all combine perfectly and don't overpower or take over the flavor of the burgers. The restaurant is family owned and the staff are always friendly. Anytime I recommend this place to family/friends they never disappoint.

The best halal platter in town!! The lamb and chicken were both tender and they serve big pieces. I seriously went there twice in one day 😅 The owners are soooo nice. I got a discount the second time around which was so sweet. One mile from my work so I'll be back all the time. Thanks again!

New food truck tucked behind the shell gas station so its easy to miss. Great smash burgers, tater tots, NYC style gyro plates:  (seasoned rice bedding with choice of seasoned chicken or lab meat, side of lettuce and a drizzle of white mayo/yogurt sauce and their naga spicy sweet bbq style sauce)

name: NYC Halal Eats
formatted_address: 5377 12 Mile Rd, Warren, MI 48092, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM

top_reviews.text:
I’m extremely disappointed with my experience at NYC Halal Eats.

I ordered food from here yesterday for my child. While feeding her, I noticed something in the fish that looked like a parasite or worm. You can imagine how disturbing that is when you’re serving food to your kid.

I immediately went back to the restaurant and asked to speak with the manager. The employees told me the manager wasn’t there, took my number, and said he would call me. When he finally did, he told me to come back the next day at 5 PM.

I came back exactly when he asked, and once again the manager was not there. Instead, the staff showed me an uncooked fish and tried to explain that the thing I found was a “fish vein.” Whether that’s true or not, I didn’t eat the food and my child was already being fed it when I discovered it. I simply asked for a refund.

Instead of resolving the situation, they told me to wait another 10 minutes for the manager, as if my time didn’t matter. After already coming back once because they asked me to, I felt completely disrespected as a regular customer.

Food safety and customer service should be taken seriously especially when a customer finds something questionable in their food. The way this was handled was unprofessional and frustrating.

Very disappointing experience.

Okay but this crispy chicken sandwich with tenders and fries was soooo good 😍
Went there around lunch time today. The chicken was perfectly crispy outside and super tender inside , you can tell it was fresh and high quality. Fries were hot, crispy, and not greasy at all.

Big shoutout to the chef, because everything was seasoned and cooked perfectly 👏🏽
Plus, the restaurant was really clean, which I always appreciate.
Definitely a meal I’d order again without thinking 🔥🍔🍟

This food was amazing! My husband and I just found this little gem, and let me tell you—after the first visit, we doubled back the very next day like two people with no self-control. 🤣

Not only is the food top-tier, but the customer service is chef’s kiss too. We dined in today and OMGGG… the food looked so good I swear my diet tried to pack its bags and walk out. 😂

Definitely a 10 out of 10. Highly recommend—just don’t blame me when you keep coming back! 🍽️🔥

I've had the pleasure of dining at NYC Halal Eats on multiple occasions, and each time, I've been impressed by the exceptional service and consistently high-quality food. The staff's attentiveness and genuine interest in ensuring customer satisfaction are truly commendable. During my most recent visit, the employees went above and beyond, regularly checking in on our table to ensure everything met our expectations. The menu offers an impressive variety of options, catering to diverse tastes and cravings. Additionally, the cleanliness and upkeep of the establishment are noteworthy. I highly recommend NYC Halal Eats and look forward to returning soon. I'm confident that anyone who visits will have a similarly positive experience.

I can’t say enough good things about NYC Halal Eats in Warren! Every time I visit, the food is fresh, flavorful, and just hits the spot—especially the mixed platter, which is my absolute favorite. The portions are generous and the sauces are amazing (seriously, don’t skip the white sauce!). The staff always greet me with a smile and make me feel welcome, and the service is quick even when they’re busy. The place is clean and has a nice, relaxed vibe. If you’re craving delicious halal food, this is definitely the spot to try. Highly recommend!

top_reviews.text: Some of the best chopped cheese sandwiches I’ve ever had. Everything was full of flavor and the portion sizes were amazing. Definitely a great spot for a quick lunch or grab a fish platter for dinner. You won’t regret it.
Absolutely AMAZING! Food is top notch. The vibes in the restaurant are absolutely amazing. I had the BBQ Chicken Fajitas and loaded Salito Fries. Best meal I’ve had so far in Farmington Hills! 💯💯

We tried this NYC halal eats in Farmington Hills and everything was so good. We ordered the chicken Philly, chicken fajita, 2 fish platters, and a double cheeseburger, and every plate was loaded and full of flavor. Portions are very generous and definitely worth it. One thing we loved was that they have Jarritos. You honestly do not see that around much, so that was a huge plus for us. The food was fresh, hot, and really hit the spot. Customer service was also great. Everyone was friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better. You can tell they care about the food and the people coming in.
Highly recommend if you are craving good halal food with solid portions and great service.
The food and customer service was great! My favorite was the Salito fries and Oreo milkshake.

I ordered the mix platter and was really disappointed. The food came cold, and the bag and container were soaked in oil. It actually looked pretty good and the flavor wasn’t bad, but that didn’t make up for how unfresh and messy everything felt. This one completely missed the mark.

name: Halal Desi Gyro Restaurant
formatted_address: 4150 15 Mile Rd, Sterling Heights, MI 48310, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
If you’re looking for a quick, satisfying meal, this halal platter hits the spot! The meat is flavorful and well-seasoned, the rice is cooked just right, and the mix of white and hot sauces adds a bold, creamy kick. The fresh lettuce and veggies give it a nice crunch and balance out the richness.

It is a bit heavy on the sauce, which can make it messy, but if you’re hungry and want something fast and filling, this definitely delivers. Ask for “light sauce” if you prefer a cleaner presentation. Solid 4 stars!

All of the food was delicious. NYC Style Lamb Gyro is only $8.99 and it comes on a plate that's big enough to feed two people. The desi wrap was also pretty good.

Better deals here than in Costco. Highly recommend

Today, I dined in with two other family members and the restaurant was completely empty. Despite that, the service and food quality were still incredibly disappointing. We ordered Chicken 65 with rice, 3-way noodles, and 2 orders of the small chicken wraps. I’ve given this location multiple chances, but they've let me down every single time. It seems like it’s normal at this location to serve people old food instead of what people actually paid for. I am not paying to eat yesterday’s leftovers. I’ve noticed many reviews mentioning the same issues, and I've personally experienced this during my previous two visits as well.
Not only did it take over 30 minutes for my order to be ready, but the meal itself was very underwhelming. The noodles (as shown in the photo below) were old, cold, and had a bitter aftertaste, like they’d been sitting around for a day or two. I could immediately tell the moment I opened the box that the noodles were a day or two old by the looks and smell of it. The rice on the Chicken 65 was old, and they were very stingy with the chicken portion as well.
When I thought things couldn’t get worse, the wrap that was supposed to come with homemade ranch (as it always has, based on both the website and my past experiences) instead had Sriracha, which didn’t go well with the dry, poorly seasoned chicken. In other words the chicken tasted like chicken. Based on this visit, I unfortunately won’t be returning and would advise you to think twice before visiting.

Great food. Like really good food. But they hours are never right. I just drove to their restaurant with 4 hours left in their hours of operation.

They are closed. This has happened 3 times. Don't go here if you are hungry as they might be closed.
Love this spot! Their NY-style chicken gyro rice is a must-try. Amazing value too - the $8.99 single serving is generous enough for 2 people!

name: Coney Star Halal Kitchen
formatted_address: 13347 Conant, Detroit, MI 48212, USA
hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours

top_reviews.text:
I was in Detroit for the weekend and craving a good gyro. I found this spot through Google and decided to give it a try — even though it was a 39-minute drive, it was totally worth it!

I ordered the gyro and a Philly cheesesteak with extra grilled jalapeños — both were packed with flavor. The Philly was a solid 10/10. The fries were also crispy and tasty.

It took about 15–20 minutes for the food to be ready, but it was definitely worth the wait. The place isn’t fine dining, but it’s clean with decent seating. It’s not really a family-style environment, more of a casual quick-bite spot. There’s also some parking available behind the store.

I also tried the chicken roll — delicious! Highly recommend this place if you’re in the area and craving flavorful fast food.

Yeah nah this place is so good.
This is the best chicken over rice I've ever had.
The Tikka naga flavor with extra meat is definitely the way to go. And It's 24/7. unbelievable. Reasonable price. Good customer service too. 10/10 rec.

My husband and I came to this restaurant tonight for the first time he got the fried chicken breast and waffles which was fire The chicken breast was seasoned so good and tender the waffles were so sweet We also got a mix of the extra large Kool-Aid and I got a halal Coney which was amazing and he got another dog which was pretty spicy but he said he liked it. And we have not dug into the five pack of egg rolls yet but they look phenomenal

Coney Star Halal Kitchen in Detroit is truly a hidden gem. I’ve tried the majority of their menu, and everything has been exceptional. The food is always fresh, flavorful, and perfectly seasoned — you can tell they take pride in every dish they serve.

Whether it’s their classic Coney favorites or their halal specialties, the quality is consistently top-tier. The portions are generous, the prices are fair, and the service is warm and welcoming every time.

If you’re looking for a spot that delivers great taste, great value, and great hospitality, Coney Star Halal Kitchen is absolutely worth the visit. Highly recommended!

The staff's service was poor and they didn't pay attention to the customer. I went to pick up my order and the employee told me I had to wait an additional four minutes. I waited for about nine minutes, then she came back to tell me I had to wait another minute. I waited three minutes and that employee left, only for another employee to tell me she was preparing my order. I waited another three minutes and that employee came out to tell me she wasn't sure about my order and wasn't sure when it would be ready. It seemed I had to wait another 10 to 15 minutes. They wasted my time and disappointed me. I will not go back to this place again and I don't recommend visiting it.

name: My Thai Halal Kitchen
formatted_address: 4116 15 Mile Rd, Sterling Heights, MI 48310, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

top_reviews.text:
Got the sampler to try everything and it was delicious. I usually hate spring rolls but this one wasn’t too crunchy on the inside. The crab rangoon had yummy cream cheese inside. The chicken satay was like sweet chicken on a stick. The fried chicken was my favorite, perfect amount of salt on the skin. The dumpling had veggies inside. We also got shrimp momos, which were my favorite out of everything. They were chewy in the best way and the shrimp was so good, plus the sauce was amazing.

We took another order of shrimp momos, the drunken noodles with crab and shrimp, the chicken noodle soup, and the tom yum soup To Go. Everything was salty and flavorful, not sweet, which I really appreciated since I don’t like sweet dishes. We also got Thai tea and taro bubble tea and both were delicious. They even had Salaam Cola, which is always nice to see at a halal restaurant.

The place was really pretty. Sit in the back booths to see the pictures on the doors since the menu doesn’t have photos.

My Thai Halal Kitchen offers tasty, fresh and Halal food. This was my 4th visit in 12 months here and the food quality is the same.
Kow Pad, Bangkok Chicken, Sesame Chicken and fries are my staple orders from here.

Do let me know what to try here.

For someone like me, who likes spices and well balanced flavors My Thai Halal is a great spot.

Omg! The food was delicious and hit the spot, but what really made the visit special was the outstanding service. The staff goes above and beyond to make you feel welcome. A special shout-out to Farah, who was so nice and attentive throughout our meal. Highly recommend ❤️. You can see the damage here 😅

I placed an online order for the pineapple curry fried rice, and it was absolutely delicious! The flavor was rich, the ingredients were fresh, and the portion was great. My order was prepared quickly and everything was still hot and flavorful when it arrived. Smooth online ordering, great food… I’ll definitely be ordering again!

Update: The owner reached out and made it right. I love that they take their customer experience seriously. I still order from them because the food is great.

I am saddened to write this review because I love this restaurant but I ordered my usual, which is the my thai fried rice. It comes with shrimp, chicken and beef.. and sadly upon opening my bowl there is barely any shrimp in the bowl. Even the chicken and beef which they usually put a lot of is very small and barely noticeable. I love thai food and used to go to Bangkok thai, then switched over to go sy thai before I found these guys and up until today,  their food has been superb but I am extremely disappointed now. See photos attached. It looks like they cut the shrimp into bits and pieces; which is awful and unlike them.

name: Halal Eatery
formatted_address: 3822 Oakwood, Melvindale, MI 48122, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM

top_reviews.text: First time here for my husband and I. The food was fresh and tasty. You can definitely tell they are smoking meat in the back which i enjoyed but it may not be pleasant for others if they're not expecting it, so just saying if it bothers you please order to-go. There are also only 2 booths inside so keep that in mind. The space is small but hits the nail on the head with the food. The bowls were great portion size and everything was super fresh. I love that they made such a good middle eastern food spot in melvindale.

I had an amazing dining experience here! This new spot is now our family go-to. My brothers and I tried most of their menu offerings: the Halal Eatery Burger (with smoked brisket!), the Cheetos Burger (it works!), Smoked wings, and naked wings (the staff was incredibly kind and let us try various sauces). And, hands down, THE best loaded chicken fries I've ever had in my life!! Everything was cooked to perfection and was presented beautifully. Burgers, Shawarma, everything, Each bite was bursting with flavor!
It's clear that the owners and staff put a lot of love and care into crafting their recipes, and the service was top-notch. Prices are more than reasonable. You must try this place! I can't praise them enough— the food here is uniquely delicious!

I had a terrific time at Halal Eatery, me and a couple friends stopped by for an afternoon bite, and we enjoyed the Grilled Chicken Bowls, the Smoked Chicken Bowls, and the Hot Cheetos Burger. All of them tasted well, no complaints! Jihad gave us a really smooth, impeccable customer service experience, checked up on us during our dine in several times, and gave us napkins without asking. Super generous guy. The owner was also very kind and gave us generous portions with our rice bowls, and was humorous and entertaining to talk to. Had a great afternoon lunch here, highly recommend! Give it a shot!

This place is awesome! The people rock! Shout out to Jason for helping my friend out big time. He took the time out of his busy schedule to run out and help my handicap friend with an order. Even ran the food out to my guy when everything was finished. Appreciate the awesome service. God bless! 🙏

Halal Eatery is a hidden gem! The food was incredibly delicious, full of flavor, and clearly made with care. Everything was fresh, portions were great, and the staff was welcoming and professional. Clean place, great service, and amazing halal food. I’ll definitely be coming back again and again!Halal Eatery is a hidden gem! The food was incredibly delicious, full of flavor, and clearly made with care. Everything was fresh, portions were great, and the staff was welcoming and professional. Clean place, great service, and amazing halal food. I’ll definitely be coming back again and again!

name: Ghazal Al Furat
formatted_address: 34726 Dequindre Rd, Sterling Heights, MI 48310, USA
hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
Ghazal al Furat is a truly exceptional restaurant that deserves every bit of praise and support. The quality of the food is outstanding everything tastes incredibly fresh, authentic, and clearly prepared with passion. It's easily some of the best Iraqi cuisine in the area.

What truly elevates the experience is the service. The owners, staff, and management are all exceptionally polite, kind, and attentive, making you feel genuinely welcome from the moment you step inside. This level of hospitality, combined with the excellent cooking, makes Ghazal al Furat a must-visit. They are a wonderful local business that deserves all the success. Highly, highly recommended!

I’m giving Ghazal Alfurat three stars, mainly because the food is the strongest part of the experience. The dishes were decent, flavorful, and clearly made with care.

Unfortunately, the overall experience is brought down by several major issues. The atmosphere is very poor. The restaurant looks outdated, like it’s stuck in the 90s, and cleanliness is a serious concern. The dining area feels dirty, and the washrooms were especially filthy, which is unacceptable for any restaurant.

Another serious concern is that payments seems to only be collected in cash, with nothing being punched into the register, simply communicating the total verbally and no receipt provided. There was no visible record of the transaction at all. This lack of transparency is very concerning and unprofessional, especially for customers who expect proper billing and accountability.

Service started off okay, but things took a very uncomfortable turn. The owners got into a loud argument with another customer in the middle of the restaurant. What made it worse was the use of Arabic cussing words, spoken loudly in front of other diners, including families with children. This was extremely disrespectful and unprofessional. Watching this unfold made many people visibly uncomfortable, especially parents who were dining with their kids.

A restaurant should be a welcoming and respectful environment. While the food has potential, the poor atmosphere, lack of cleanliness, and inappropriate behavior make it hard to recommend without serious improvements.
Best most authentic Iraqi place in Michigan! I loved the food so much and staff so nice friendly and funny!
Had an amazing meal here. Everything tastes fresh and the beef kabob was some of the best I have had. I got the half order and it was an amazing value. I will definitely be back soon!!!

Very friendly and nice owner, he will serve you by himself with a smile and very nice attitude.
The food was good I didn’t try a lot of plates but I received a lot of good feedback from friends about his food.
Try it yourself.

top_reviews.text: The building was hella cold, could’ve left the door open. The lady was hardly any help, I even took into consideration that she didn’t speak very good English. But that doesn’t matter when your actions aren’t even welcoming. The lo mein I had was okay, thus why I gave 2 stars. But the samosa were dry, the meat was pink, there was too much Indian seasoning, the sauces given were disgusting. There were no napkins no plastic ware. Won’t be coming back, this was a complete waste of money 🙄😑she literally couldn’t tell us anything.
Food was delicious, service was good! Their noodles and soups are highly recommended. Loved their butter chicken as well.

Run……..
If you are really hungry don’t even think about this place. Food quality is so bad.I order 4 different items.all off 4 items are nasty food that I cud-not put second spun in my mouth.And mango lassi please don’t order this if you do you gonna have problem in you stomach.
So save your time and your money go somewhere else
It’s was really amazing lunch we had at this restaurant food were delicious and good quality great service nice environment for family. Recommend ..

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Review for Halal Chinese Indo-Pak Restaurant

Absolutely outstanding experience!
From the moment we walked in, we felt the warmth, kindness, and hospitality that is rare to find these days.

We were served an unforgettable spread —
Chicken Manchurian, beef dishes, fresh salads, tea, and their delicious mango juice —
each item was cooked with such flavor, balance, and love that it truly felt like a meal with barakah in every bite.

The Chicken Manchurian was tender and perfectly seasoned,
the beef dishes were rich and full-bodied,
and even the tea had that homemade touch that reminds you of true Indo-Pak–Chinese fusion.

What stood out even more was the courtesy and manners of the staff, who treated us like family rather than customers.
Their service was warm, prompt, and filled with genuine smiles.

A huge thank you to Shahed Bhai and his wonderful team for creating such a welcoming environment and serving food that nourishes both the stomach and the heart.

Highly recommended for families, travelers, and anyone who loves flavorful halal food made with sincerity and skill.
This place is a gem in Sterling, Michigan.

The food is a MUST! The Birria tacos and burrito bowl were full to the top and delicious.

top_reviews.text: The sharkora beef entree with fresh naan roti was delicious. Great service and beautiful atmosphere. Best restaurant in Hamtramck for family seating and eating out.
Had a beef Donor kabab. Tasted so good. The cafe environment has different vibe. Food quantity was way enough price wise.

Great food! Service was excellent by Anika.
Highly recommended. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I know the owner, and he takes pride in the food. We run an urban farm adjacent to the restaurant in the back of Masjid. The delicious food stands out as different from your regular Bengali restaurant. Everyone should try it. Highly recommend. You can see our farm afterward.

We ordered the beef biryani and beef cilantro. Someone else mentioned the biryani tasted like muri satni and that is absolutely true unfortunately. Also I saw a comment that fresh organic vegetables were used in the dishes but I could tell the beef cilantro had the mixed frozen vegetables used in it with maybe 3 slices of bell pepper. Also the masala chai tasted like black tea and it cost 3 dollars. We were unsatisfied with the food, especially for the price paid.

name: Halal Cafe
formatted_address: 13736 Linwood St, Detroit, MI 48238, USA
hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 3:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Absolutely Delicious!

Hala Café never disappoints! I ordered the beef sausage, beef bacon, potatoes and onions, egg whites, grits, and toast, and everything was cooked to perfection. The beef sausage was juicy with just the right amount of spice, and the beef bacon had that perfect smoky crisp that melts in your mouth. The potatoes and onions were golden, flavorful, and seasoned beautifully.

The egg whites were fluffy and fresh — clearly made with care — and the grits were creamy, smooth, and comforting, just like good Southern cooking should be. The toast tied it all together with that nice buttery crunch.

Every bite tasted homemade, hearty, and full of love. If you’re looking for a breakfast that’s both wholesome and satisfying, this combo is a must-try! 👏🔥

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Excellent Breakfast at Halal Cafe!

I stopped by Halal Cafe for breakfast and was really impressed with the quality and flavor of everything I ordered. The beef sausages were perfectly seasoned — juicy and packed with flavor without being too salty. The beef bacon had a great smoky taste and a nice crisp to it, a delicious alternative to regular bacon.

The hash browns were golden and crunchy on the outside, soft inside — just how they should be. I also loved the egg whites, which were cooked light and fluffy, and paired perfectly with the toast that came warm and buttery.

Everything tasted fresh, well-prepared, and you can tell they use quality ingredients. It’s not easy finding a good halal breakfast spot, but Halal Cafe nailed it. Definitely coming back for another round soon!

If you're looking for some good eating with No Pork? Go to Hahlal Cafe! They don't serve  "Swine of any kind" but they have chicken, turkey and, beef bacon, salmon croquettes with hash browns or grits and eggs. All there meals are on point and delicious! I always get my 5 wing with fries special or wing dings. It's 3 blocks off Davidson on Linwood. Open daily till 6 except Monday,  when they're closed. Cash and Cashapp accepted.
Everything was excellent. A good Mom and Pop spot. Their slogan is Halal Cafe where everyone knows your name.

I had the best salmon croquettes I’ve ever had in my life. They were moist inside and crispy out. Just delicious. Eggs with cheddar cooked perfectly. Grits were creamy and of great consistency. The German chocolate cake was decadent and rich and just the right amount of icing. I will definitely be back to explore the rest of the menu. Oh, they have
bean pie some days. A rare treat.

Food is great! Love that the food was made to order. Hard to find these days. Small 5 min wait, food came out hot and fresh. Chicken sandwich with mild seasoning was perfect.

We will be back again.

Absolutely amazing food at Khan’s Hot Chicken! I ordered the shrimp over rice, and it was incredible. The shrimp were perfectly cooked, full of flavor, and the rice was seasoned really well. name: Khan’s Hot Chicken
formatted_address: 28893 Bunert Rd, Warren, MI 48088, USA

top_reviews.text:
🔥 Just tried Khan’s Hot Chicken (28893 Bunert Rd, Warren, MI 48088) and I’ve got to say—it’s my new go-to spot in Warren! The sliders, tenders, and rice platters were packed with flavor, portions were huge, and the spice level was just how I like it. Plus, the staff were super friendly.
👉 Easy to find: from 12 Mile & Hayes, head south on Hayes, turn right on Bunert—it’s right there.
If you love bold, tasty food, I definitely recommend checking it out!

Food has always tasted the best. I’ve had multiple halal chicken sandwich places from here to Chicago, and across all Khan’s Hot Chicken is easily the best tasting. It’s not excessively greasy like some places are, and you can always taste how fresh/juicy the chicken is. Consistently good, and a personal favorite for me and a lot of friends. (Personally recommend the #2 and #6 on the menu)

The guys who work here are great and always friendly and accommodating, really appreciate them as well. Overall would give it more than 5 stars if I could.

Also really appreciate the HFSAA Halal Certification (strictest Halal certification available) which gives ease of mind about everything on the menu always being zabiha

Khan’s Hot Chicken is hands down one of my favorite restaurants. Every dish is packed with flavor the lamb and chicken bowls, gyros, tenders, and sliders are all incredible. Beyond the amazing food, the staff is always friendly and welcoming, some of the best people I know making every visit feel special. The atmosphere is comfortable and inviting, the kind of place where you can relax and just enjoy your meal. Highly recommend this spot to anyone —amazing food and great service! Definitely coming back.

Great food! The loaded fries and chicken & rice bowl are my go to. So many flavors! My roommate goes there every Friday. Been there many times over the past year, and not one complaint about the food. Highly recommend!

top_reviews.text:
I can’t believe I spent $15 on this chicken. I usually buy my chicken from here and have always been satisfied, but this time was a complete disappointment. I asked for 2 lbs of organic chicken, and they said they only had 2.5 lbs so I agreed, trusting their quality.

Not only was the chicken frozen but I had also asked for small pieces, and they gave uneven chunks instead. If I wanted frozen chicken in big pieces, I could’ve easily gotten it elsewhere for much cheaper. Really let down by the service and quality this time especially from a place I’ve always trusted.

I’ve been a regular at Namaste Fresh Meat for their good selection of meat and seafood, especially for their Pomfret. We usually place our order over the phone, and when I go to pick it up, the wait time is typically very minimal. But last week, when I went to pick up my order, there was a slight hiccup, it wasn’t ready, or they may have missed it. No worries, though! Within 10 minutes or less, they had it all ready. They were quick, and the Pomfret was cut into perfectly sized pieces, just as I requested.

A helpful tip: paying in cash gets you a discount, while there’s a 4% extra charge for card payments. Also, this place is usually quite busy, and I’ve never seen it without a crowd. So, it’s always best to place your order in advance to avoid any delays.

Situated in a busy strip mall with shared parking, Namaste Fresh Meat might have a small storefront, but it’s famous for the quality of its products. Everything is prepared in the back of the store, and when you go for pickup, it’s brought out to you. The staff cuts everything to your exact needs, and they make sure it’s nicely trimmed. All meats are hand-cut to order, which ensures freshness and quality.

The prices here are competitive, and the service is fast and reliable. If you’re looking for a place that offers fresh, quality meat and seafood, Namaste Fresh Meat is definitely worth the visit. Just be sure to plan ahead, especially since it’s always a popular spot! Overall, Great quality, excellent service, and competitive prices. Just make sure to place your order in advance to save time!

I always call them and tell that chicken is not fresh and bad smell and I did this more than two times and this time I would  like to write this review. top_reviews.text:
Abo Halal Market (formerly known as Abdu Market) is my go-to place for fresh meat and a great selection of Asian - Indian, and Pakistani groceries. It’s important to note that this store is not related to the other Abdu Market in Michigan—both are entirely separate businesses.

I usually come here for their fresh meat, and they always chop it to the size I request. The process is seamless—I just call ahead to place my order, and it’s always ready for pickup when I arrive. All I have to do is walk in and pay. Super convenient!

Beyond meat, they carry a good variety of groceries, including spices, rice, lentils, and other essentials. They also have fresh snacks, which is a nice bonus. The store is clean, well-organized, and welcoming. The staff is friendly, both in person and over the phone, always greeting customers with warmth. Parking is shared with other stores in the plaza, but I’ve never had trouble finding a spot.

Overall, Abo Halal Market is a great choice for quality meat, a diverse grocery selection, and excellent customer service.

top_reviews.text: Fresh - Delicious - Affordable - A true hidden treasure in Lansing. A must stop for the smoothest coffee, slightly sweetened, to perfectly accompany the flakiest baklava I’ve found in town. Follow that up with this moist coconut pastry not to be found anywhere else. Don’t miss their take on pizza - where flavors collide on a pillow of fresh bread. I can’t say enough about the quality of food found here but more importantly the quality of hospitality. We always go shop from her store whenever we are in Lansing although our house is 12 miles away just because she’s the best and her food is really halal for those who care about halal meat and chicken!

The hospitality of both owners was beyond what I expected. I got to taste the pizza and the chicken on it was so fresh and light. I will return as soon as I can. These owners are the nicest people perhaps I've ever met.  We wandered in and each time, they've been warm and inviting, as if we were walking into their home.  They have the most delicious pastries and their signature "pizza" flatbread varieties are awesome.  The meat is flawless, and everything on their shelves is fresh and delicious.  You can tell they have a passion for helping people.  My fiancee has walked out of there crying each time she's been because of how nice they are.  It's almost hard to understand in the world we live in.  A truly one of a kind place in Lansing.

name: Noorjahan Indian Cuisine (Detroit)
formatted_address: 107 Monroe St, Detroit, MI 48226, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 3:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
First of all, I want to apologize for leaving a negative review. I usually avoid writing bad reviews unless it’s absolutely necessary. If you check my profile, you’ll see that I rarely do this. However, I’ve heard several negative comments about this business (from other locations, not this one), and unfortunately, my experience aligns with them.

I visited this location to try the lunchbox they advertise for $9.99. It looked appealing in the advertisement, and I was hopeful. I have no complaints about the cleanliness, the location, or the staff—the employees were genuinely friendly and welcoming.

However, the real issue was the food. The lunchbox I received was honestly disappointing. It felt like it was made from leftovers—the curries were nearly empty, and what I got was mostly just gravy with little to no meat or vegetables. I had selected the items myself, but it didn’t seem to matter.

Many people walk 10–15 minutes from their workplace in downtown to get lunch here. When the food served is of such poor quality, it feels like a waste of time and money. If you’re unable to offer decent portions or fresh food at this price point, it might be better to either improve the quality or reconsider the offering entirely.

That said, I do wish the best for the business and its staff, and I hope they take this feedback constructively. Good luck to you all.

Overall, it was a great experience. It was an amazing sit-down or grab-and-go experience. Dining included high-quality cutlery and fancy dishes. Portion sizes were generous. The spice levels were very accurate for spicy lovers and mild murraders alike. And the staff is very friendly and informative if you have questions or need a step in the right direction.

The food was heavenly, as usual. Ms. Oli made the mint sauce herself. Loved it!! I had a lot of requests and she was very patient and sweet as pie. This place is in the heart of downtown and the vibe was so relaxing yet fun. With the music and my tastebuds being painted with authentic flavors of India I mentally escaped and just had a great solo date. My heart beats for all the local authentic places.❤️
Friendly staff food authentic taste and located in heart of downtown. My go to place for Indian food in Detroit.

Food and service are a definite 10/10, even higher. This was my first visit and the young lady was super helpful with the questions I had with the menu. Super kind and patient, and the food was just lovely! A warm and inviting place, next time I visit Detroit this will be one of my first stops, no doubt!

hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
This Indian store was recommended by a friend mainly for their homemade chapati, and I have to say, they didn’t disappoint. I usually call ahead to check if they have fresh chapati available and then stop by. I’ve purchased them twice so far, and both times they were fresh, soft, and tasted just like homemade.

That said, there was one occasion when my friend picked up chapati for me and they were a bit hard, so it may be a hit or miss depending on how fresh the batch is. The service has always been friendly, with a warm welcome every time I visit. The store itself is clean and well maintained, which makes the experience pleasant.

I haven’t personally shopped much for groceries or snacks yet. From a quick glance, the snack variety seemed somewhat limited. Parking is convenient, with a good-sized lot shared with nearby stores.

Overall, a decent stop, especially if you call ahead and are looking for fresh homemade chapati for travel.

I purchased Britannia Digestive from this store on Christmas Eve, only to discover a dirty oil smell when I opened the packet a week later. To my surprise, the product was two months past its expiry date.

Be cautious about what you buy and to always check the expiry date before purchasing.

Giving a one star so that the store manager ensures quality going forwards.

Great place for Indian and Asian grocery shopping! The service is amazing, and the vegetables are always fresh. They offer a wonderful variety of quality products, and we’re very happy to have this store in our neighborhood. Thank you for continuing to serve the community with such great food and excellent quality!
They have good ambience and we liked their Thursday night buffet
Very friendly staff. They were very much engaged in helping me find what I was looking for .

top_reviews.text: This grocery store is stocked with almost everything you might need. The staff are polite and helpful, which makes shopping here a pleasant experience. The location is convenient too. I was especially happy to see that they also had beautiful Ganesh idols available..

This store has been constantly selling stale or expired products, especially chips and snack items. Many of the packages do not have any expiry or best-by dates, and the ones that do are mostly past their date.

When I brought it up, the store staff claimed it’s the customer’s responsibility to check for expiry dates before purchase—but how can you do that when there’s no date on the package at all?

This is a serious concern, especially for anyone with a sensitive gut or food sensitivities. I would not recommend shopping here if you care about food safety or product quality. I had to argue for long time to get a refund. However i am personally going to report it to Michigan Health department to see their take on this.

top_reviews.text: I visited the most wonderful grocery store today. It was a Yemeni grocery store with the strongest most pleasant aroma of spices, compassion, joy, and peace. Check out the endless variety in this drink aisle! 🥤 Al-Haramain in Farmington Hills is a must-visit. They have the best deals on fresh fruits and vegetables, delicious bakery items, and a massive selection of spices. The staff is always so friendly and helpful! 🙌🍎🥐"

Al-Haramain International Foods

Location: 3351 E 12 Mile Rd, Warren, MI 48092

Overall Impression

Al-Haramain International Foods is a standout destination for Middle Eastern and international groceries in the Metro Detroit area. Located on 12 Mile Road in Warren, it's a bustling, well-stocked market that serves as a vital resource for anyone seeking authentic ingredients, high-quality halal meats, and freshly prepared goods. It's a clean, organized, and vibrant store that offers a comprehensive shopping experience.

Highlights

Exceptional Halal Butcher Counter: This is arguably the store's main attraction. The butcher counter is known for its fresh, high-quality cuts of halal lamb, beef, and chicken. The staff is knowledgeable and can handle custom requests. The meat is consistently praised for its freshness and flavor.

In-House Bakery: The aroma of freshly baked bread is one of the first things you'll notice. Their flatbread (khubz/pita) is made fresh throughout the day and is a must-buy. It's soft, warm, and far superior to pre-packaged alternatives. They also offer a variety of other baked goods and sweets.

Vast Product Selection: The aisles are packed with a diverse range of products from across the Middle East, North Africa, and South Asia. You'll find an extensive selection of spices, grains (like freekeh and bulgur), beans, specialty rice, olive oils, cheeses (like Nabulsi and Akkawi), and a fantastic olive bar.

Fresh Produce: The produce section is reliable, offering standard fruits and vegetables alongside items that can be harder to find in conventional supermarkets, such as fresh figs, dates, and specific types of herbs and greens used in Middle Eastern cuisine.

Verdict

Al-Haramain is a top-tier international market. Whether you are planning to cook an authentic Middle Eastern meal, looking for the best quality halal meat, or simply want to stock your pantry with unique and flavorful ingredients, it is an essential destination. The combination of a fantastic butcher, a fresh bakery, and an extensive grocery selection makes it a one-stop shop and a true gem in the community.
Great price and amazing collection of food, meat, fruits

Purchased a birthday cake from their bakery. Cut it open on the same day of purchase and the bottom of the cake was covered in mold. I returned it back to the store where they refunded me after I had to show them multiple pieces of cut cake covered in green mold. No apologies. Staff acted as if it was no big deal. Nothing. Will never set foot in this store again

top_reviews.text:
Nichole did an amazing job officiating our wedding! She is so gifted at writing and made our audience feel like she's known us for decades. Overall I can't imagine having anyone else officiant one of the best days of our lives!

We are so thankful to Nichole Bertucci for officiating our wedding so beautifully. From start to finish, she was warm, professional, and truly attentive to our story. The ceremony she delivered was heartfelt, meaningful, and perfectly reflected who we are as a couple. Many of our guests commented on how touching and personal it was. Nichole brought such a calm and joyful presence to our day, and we couldn't have asked for a better officiant. We highly recommend her to any couple looking for someone who truly cares and goes above and beyond. Thank you, Nichole, for making our ceremony so unforgettable!

From beginning to end Jena exceeded our expectations! She guided us through the process, answered all of our questions and even got on board as we changed the venue the night before which required her to rewrite the ceremony. The best thing she brought to our special day was how positive and encouraging she was! Thank you SO much for being a part of our wedding day!!!

The only words I have to describe Nichole is just wow. She truly, TRULY was put on this earth to do this. Nichole does a lot of elopements, but she is PHENOMINAL at officiating large weddings. Nichole created and just absolutely nailed the most PERFECT ceremony (and I don’t say that lightly, it was seriously perfect). She has the recipe for ceremonies down to a science. I can’t tell you how many of our 170 guests just RAVED about the ceremony. Perfect length, perfect verbiage, perfect everything. We’ll never be able to go to another wedding now (unless Nichole is officiating) because no ceremony will top what she created for us. She was BEYOND professional and just so easy to work with throughout the year prior to our wedding day, we never had to worry once with her. I just don’t have any other words to describe our ceremony except perfection. We’d hire her over and over and over again without even thinking twice. Nichole, thank you SO MUCH. We will be calling you to officiate our vow renewal in 10 years! THANK YOU NICHOLE!

We absolutely loved working with Nichole! She made us feel so comfortable from the very beginning and really took the time to make the ceremony personal and meaningful. She sent us the script well in advance so we could review it, which we really appreciated. The ceremony itself was the perfect length and flowed beautifully — exactly what we had envisioned. Highly recommend!

name: American Moslem Society (Masjid Dearborn)
formatted_address: 9945 Vernor Hwy, Dearborn, MI 48120, USA
hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours

top_reviews.text: This mosque is honestly amazing. It’s spacious, beautiful, and has that historic vibe that just makes you feel at peace. The people here are super welcoming and respectful, and the whole environment feels like a real community. Everything is clean, organized, and you can tell they put a lot of care into the place. Definitely one of my favorite spots to pray.

The presence of this masjid has been a cornerstone in my life since childhood, shaping my values and understanding of community. Its doors have always been open, welcoming everyone with warmth and compassion. Growing up, I found solace in its teachings, which instilled in me a strong sense of faith and belonging. The masjid has played a pivotal role in fostering connections among diverse members of the community, offering a space for prayer, learning, and social support. Its programs not only provide spiritual guidance but also address the needs of the community, from educational initiatives to charity drives. As I reflect on my journey, I am grateful for how this masjid has nurtured my growth and continues to uplift those around it, creating a thriving environment for all.

I’ve been attending the American Muslim Society since 1996, and it has truly been a cornerstone of the Dearborn community. The masjid has continuously grown and evolved while staying true to its mission of faith, education, and service. They offer excellent programs for both youth and adults, fostering spiritual growth, community engagement, and leadership.

The school is always full, which speaks volumes about the quality of education and trust families have in this institution. The atmosphere is welcoming, the staff and volunteers are dedicated, and the sense of community here is unmatched.

Whether you’re coming for prayer, learning, or simply to connect with others, AMS is a blessing to our community.

The American Moslem Society (AMS) in Dearborn isn’t just a mosque — it’s a second home. The moment you step inside, you feel the peace, the unity, and the genuine love of the community. The imams speak straight from the heart and always remind us of what truly matters.

During Ramadan, the energy here is unmatched — the prayers, the iftars, the togetherness. You see every generation, young and old, praying side by side. It’s a beautiful reminder of what Islam is all about.

The masjid is spotless, well-organized, and full of people who care deeply about their faith and their community. AMS is a true blessing for Dearborn and beyond. May Allah reward everyone who keeps this place running so beautifully.

From the very first time I stepped into this masjid as a child, it felt like a second home. The sense of community here has always been palpable, creating bonds that transcend generations. As I grew, the teachings and values shared within these walls guided my development and shaped my character. This masjid has been a hub of activity, offering essential programs that cater to the needs of our diverse community, whether it's educational workshops, youth mentorship, or charitable initiatives. It's heartening to see how it brings people together, fostering a spirit of unity and support that enriches our lives and strengthens our ties. I truly appreciate how this place has not only influenced my upbringing but continues to be a beacon of hope and guidance for so many.

name: NoorJahan Indian Cuisine - Waterford
formatted_address: 4671 W Walton Blvd, Waterford Township, MI 48329, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.text:
I ordered from this restaurant for the 1st time today. Service was good. Restaurant was  very clean but empty on a Friday night. I ordered the chicken tikka masala, butter chicken, and garlic naan.

I must say that both chicken dishes are EXTREMELY sweet…it’s very obvious that a sweetener was used for both sauces and they used too much. I’ve had Indian food many times and have never had dishes so sweet. They almost taste like a pizza sauce.

There is an option for extra chicken (which I added for the butter chicken). I understand why now, the dishes do not have a lot of chicken in them…plenty of sauce though.

I wouldn’t try these dishes again if I ever go back.

I ordered the Lamb Tikka Masala, Garlic Naan, and Chicken Tandoori. The Tandoori was so moist and tender, and the Lamb was just as good. Dipping the naan into the Masala sauce? Absolutely delicious—chef’s kiss! My friends tried other dishes and loved them too. The portions are huge and very affordable—we all left with happy, full bellies and still had plenty to take home. I’m already looking forward to lunch tomorrow!

My friends and I are travel nurses, and we all agree this place is a “must come back” spot. I can’t wait to try more dishes, though honestly, it might be hard to top what I had. Mahi, the owner, is super sweet, and the atmosphere is really comfortable—music was a little loud, but not a dealbreaker. Overall, an excellent experience. This is definitely going to be our new favorite Indian restaurant while we’re in town!

I ordered the butter chicken lunchbox and it blew me away. The portions are extremely generous — a bottomless serving of soft, fluffy basmati rice, perfectly spiced chana masala packed with tender chickpeas, and big chunks of chicken swimming in the richest, smoothest butter chicken sauce I’ve had in a long time.

The flavor is incredible: the butter chicken is velvety and comforting, the chana adds a bold and earthy contrast, and together they make a perfect combo plate. Everything tasted fresh, warm, and deeply satisfying.

If you're craving Indian comfort food done right, The Lunchbox is 100% worth it. I’ll definitely be ordering again.
Waiter was very helpful with recommendations since it was our first time having Indian cuisine! The Naan was ove fired in the clay oven and my Lamb Saag was delicious!

I will highly recommend to try their food and i will surely go back to try some more of their food

Friday Special Beema’s mandi Goat Biryani Review

Honestly, it felt like neither a true biryani nor a traditional mandi. The portion size was very good it included an egg, a couple of chicken pieces, mutton pieces, and a decent amount of rice enough to comfortably fill two people.

However, the gravy tasted average, and the meat pieces were a bit hard instead of tender. The rice flavor also didn’t feel like authentic Hyderabadi biryani; it tasted more like regular masala rice.

On the positive side, the service was good and there are plenty of options on the menu. We also tried a couple of drinks lassi and chickoo both house-made and excellent in taste.

Overall, good portions and drinks, but the biryani itself could be improved

All the dishes were absolutely delicious — from the flavorful curries and perfectly baked naan to the biryani platter and kebabs, everything tasted fresh and well-prepared.
Mushrooms are a bit undercooked so kinda had a sticky texture which wasn’t pleasant while eating.
However, the food was delivered a bit late, and the place felt quite busy and a little too rushy, so it’s not the most peaceful spot if you’re looking for a calm dining experience. Still, great food overall, just be prepared for a crowd.

This place is a hidden gem! The food was amazing! I tried the Besawada Lamb Pulav and my daughter got the Lamb Saag and we shared the garlic naan. Both dishes were delicious and authentic and the naan was so good. The staff was very friendly and accommodating.  I will definitely be coming back and would recommend if you're looking for great Indian cuisine.  5 🌟

Restaurant vibe is nice, food was okay, but our three dishes weren’t exactly as expected. My channa masala was good, but not exactly the typical flavor I expect, and all three dishes had crunchy, unpleasant spices hiding in them. Not pleasant to bite into. We didn’t like being served bottled water to the table rather than tap water with glasses. I won’t return.

My time here was well spent for a friendly lunch!!! I had asked for a few suggestions from the waiter and finally branched away from the typical butter chicken. I tried the methi chicken and my friend had the chicken karma. Both were scrumptious ans the Mango lassi was perfect to wash it down with. The naan is magical. Fluffy and crispy at the edges. Thank you for the awesome experience, and I hope to be back again soon!

top_reviews.text:
✈️ Just days before my wife was scheduled to fly internationally, she started experiencing sudden swelling and pain in her leg. Her primary care doctor ran tests but couldn’t pinpoint the cause—and we were seriously worried it might be DVT (Deep Vein Thrombosis), which can be life-threatening, especially on long flights.

Enter Dr. Kanaan 🙌

He got her in immediately, ran a venous Doppler to check for clots, and thankfully—no DVT. She was cleared to fly that same afternoon, and thanks to his care and prescribed meds, she was already feeling better when she landed.

We’re beyond grateful not just for the prompt and expert care, but for the peace of mind he gave her. Traveling without fear made all the difference.

hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed

top_reviews.text: I have been dealing with dental phobia for over 30 years. I’ve been through therapy and hypnosis and everything and nothing worked. I have been so many different dentists, But none were more caring than the dentist and all of the staff at Lakeside. I am so thankful to Dr. J and Carissa and everybody who worked with me. I don’t remember everyone’s names, but you know who you are. I got dentures now and they got me through the surgery and it wasn’t bad at all. I have never dealt with such a caring place, and if I had gone here years ago, my teeth would never have gotten so bad. If you’re afraid of the dentist, come to this place. They are amazing and very sensitive to your needs. Thank you all so much! My new dentures look amazing. And the pain is not bad at all.

I’m not sure where to start. So I have insurance that isn’t taken ANYWHERE, I swear. Then I found this place. They got me in within 12 hours. I had an appointment the very next morning. It was the most thorough appointment I’ve ever had. They checked absolutely everything, explained all their findings in detail that I could actually understand and the entire staff is nice, happy, smiling. I LOVE this place. I will send eeeeveryone I know here. Not to mention the poor girl had to stand there for 10 minutes while I took out my mass amounts of earrings for the X-rays and was nothing but pleasant. Amazing office. So glad I found them.

Dr Saluja is a very skilled and caring doctor. I broke a crown last fall. he removed what was left of the tooth and had to restructure the area with new bone to support an implant. Lots of grinding and drilling, but no pain. He stopped often to make sure I was ok. Waited 4 months for the new bone to heal, then inserted the implant today. Again drilling and grinding but no pain thanks to Alison and her thorough numbing of the area! The entire staff is caring, concerned and professional. I chose him initially because my wife needed extensive repair and would only do it with sedation. He treated her the same way, but this time with sedation. I would highly recommend him to anyone needing dental care.

I had put off tooth care for far too long. Came in for a consultation and they were very professional and caring, no judgment. Told me exactly what I needed to get done vs what I should get done. They are all about making you comfortable and getting you to a healthy place. As for actual cleaning and extracting, it couldn’t have gone smoother. Quick, no pain, and the post op care is by far the easiest you can imagine. They took good care of me.

I left a 5 star review, but that is not enough to express my gratitude for all of the support I've recieved from Lakeside since I began my journey with them this summer.

I FEEL LIKE ME AGAIN!

For the first time in over twenty years, I'm smiling again with confidence; I thought I never would again. I hold a public-facing role with state government and I have had to angst over the appearance of my teeth daily. It was crushing, to avoid photos with family and friends.

No more. My confidence is back, and though it has been challenging for me to learn to smile again, I'm doing it! For as long as this practice is in business (hopefully long after I'm gone), I will continue going back to them.

The front desk staff is incredibly kind and compassionate, and that continues with support staff behind the scenes. Dr. Drew is the first dentist I've ever had that genuinely wanted to help me without admonishing me first.

I trust this practice completely, and I am beyond grateful to have found them. Thanks for putting up with me, Lakeside! I look forward to seeing you again in the new year and all the years thereafter.
