top_reviews.text:
Update on my my total mouth overhaul....Dr Shah and his staff have been absolutely amazing and patient while still working with me on my overhaul.  I ended up getting a horrible infection , not because of anything done by Dr Shah and his staff it's because of my depleted immune system. My upper denture is beautiful!  My lower denture is ready and waiting for my body to recover.  I have recovered and will be getting my lower denture next week.  It too is beautiful.  BOTH fit my mouth perfectly.  BOTH are so comfortable, the color of the teeth and gums take years off of my face.  I'd like to give a special shout out to Brylyn, the kindest dental assistant I've ever had the pleasure of working with. Thank goodness, she isn't shy about people holding her hand while they are being worked on.  There's been times I was worried that I might have broken her petite hands.  She is so gentle, compassionate and understanding.  Brylyn wipes my tears as they roll down my cheeks because I'm hurting.  She coaches me along through the process and always gives me hugs afterwards.
Melody at the front desk always takes care of me when I have had to call and cancel at the last minute because of the infection I had and didn't realize that's why I was so sick.  Again, my infection had nothing to do with Dr Shah and staff.  It's my body.  Any other dental office wouldn't be as understanding or flexible.  They would have charged me.  Between Courtney, Melody and Dr Shah , they understand my financial situation, they understand my health issues and my very stressful life. I have never experienced a dental office like West Point Dental.  It's not the office, it's the people in the office that makes the difference.
Thank you Dr Shah and your entire staff.  I'm truly blessed to have you as my forever dentist.
In January, my husband will start his new insurance and he will be starting his own mouth overhaul.  I will not trust anyone other than Dr Shah and his staff to work on Mark.
I'll be seeing all of you this upcoming week.

top_reviews.text: I photographed a wedding here this year and I'm just absolutely hooked on the old money, castle-y charm of this place. It's somehow very grand and romantic but also cozy. Indianwood Golf & Country Club was the perfect setting for Carmela and Michael’s wedding! The venue is absolutely stunnin. The historic charm, beautiful architecture, and rolling golf course views created such a romantic backdrop for the day.

hours: Monday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 3:00 AM – 12:00 AM

top_reviews.text:
In response to the review written by David Smith —
It is truly disappointing to see a one-star review simply because you encountered a level of knowledge you are not yet equipped to handle. To claim that an Imam teaching in اللُّغَة الْعَرَبِيَّة (the Arabic language) is 'not from the Sunnah' is a statement born of ignorance. The Qur’an, the Sunnah, and the vast majority of Islamic scholarship were preserved in Arabic. True طُلَّاب الْعِلْم (students of knowledge) understand that to access the depths of this religion, one must strive to learn its language, rather than demanding the religion be translated down to their level of comfort.
You must also realize that you are a guest in a community where many of the congregants are native Arabic speakers. They connect deeply with the lecture in their mother tongue, and the Imam is fulfilling his duty to his community by speaking to their hearts and minds directly. Just as you desire accommodations for your lack of understanding, you must allow these brothers the right to benefit in their own language. It is the height of selfishness to walk into a gathering and demand that the entire dynamic change to suit you, effectively denying the majority their benefit.
Furthermore, there is no shortage of Masjids in this area that conduct programs strictly in English. If you require a translated experience, you have plenty of other options to choose from. However, do not come to a place known for preserving the tradition in its original tongue and complain that it does not cater to Western standards. Instead of criticizing the Imam for maintaining a high standard, you should ask yourself why you feel entitled to change a gathering of knowledge rather than putting in the effort to raise your own understanding.

I visited this temple with my class from Kuyoer College and for some reason, it felt alot like "home" to me.
My mother is a Thai Buddhist, so I grew up visiting Thai Buddhist temples. Now, I am a Catechumen in the Orthodox Church. Nevertheless, when I visited this temple, it felt nostalgic. I understand Buddhism comes from Hinduism, but the Buddhist temple near me here did not feel as nostalgic as this Hindu temple. It was an interesting experience.

When we came, the people who gave us introductions and tours were also very friendly and welcoming, and we got to see Hinduism in practice. I even held a little of a correspondence with somebody from the temple on some linguistic things (between Thai and Sanskrit).

I did not come to this temple with a heart wanting to judge, but came with a heart wanting to learn. Come with an open heart to at least learn and observe.

formatted_address: 31696 Ryan Rd, Warren, MI 48092, USA

top_reviews.text:
I recently had the opportunity to visit the Shiv Mandir - Temple of Joy in Warren, Michigan, and I was very impressed. The temple is a beautiful and peaceful place of worship, with a welcoming and inclusive atmosphere.

As soon as I arrived, I was greeted by a friendly volunteer who showed me around the temple and explained the different areas. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva, and the main shrine features a large murti of Shiva. There are also shrines to other Hindu deities, such as Ganesha, Hanuman, and Lakshmi.

I was particularly struck by the beauty of the temple's architecture and décor. The walls are adorned with intricate carvings and colorful murals, and the floors are covered in marble and tile. The temple also has a beautiful courtyard with a fountain and gardens.

I spent some time in the main shrine, meditating and praying. I found the atmosphere to be very calming and peaceful. I also had the opportunity to attend a puja ceremony, which was a truly special experience.

After the puja ceremony, I had a delicious vegetarian meal in the temple's dining hall. The food was prepared by the temple volunteers, and it was absolutely delicious.

Overall, I had a wonderful experience at the Shiv Mandir - Temple of Joy. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking

One of the most peaceful & favorite place of mine in Michigan. They have lots of cultural activities & events. Great opportunity for devotees around Detroit Hindu community to offer their prayers and get blessings. Quiet divine place for Hindu worships.🙏

Experience the Essence of Kolkata's Durga Puja Right Here! This venue impeccably organizes the Puja festivities, exceeding all expectations with their exceptional hospitality. They offer prasad in all puja event.

But that's not all – beyond Durga Puja, this place hosts puja, anjali, arati, and bhog ceremonies every Sunday throughout the year. If you're planning a special occasion like onnoprason, paite, Shraddha, birthday or any other event don't hesitate to get in touch with the management; they'll take care of all the arrangements
Nice place for prayer. We used their kitchen to cook food. Management is very helpful and kind hearted. Thank you all.

It's a very beautiful place where you can pray to god. It has ample of car parking space. There is a shoe rack on the right side when you enter the building. The lower level of the building has space to have prasad (holy food) and the upper section has a wide space to sit and pray.

Please don't forget to cover your head with shawl (for females usually) and a handkerchief (for males usually) before entering the praying section. They also provide cloth to cover your head and please return them before leaving.

Attended a religious diversity journey w/ 7th grade Michigan students. It was amazing! Everyone was welcoming to our group. Friendly. Helpful. Educational. I'm sure its a wonderful house of worship outside of the program we attended.
